spanish: Por favor, ponga las sobras en un recipiente y guárdelas en el refrigerador.

text: I have a reservation under the name Perez, and I'd like to check if my room is {{c1::ready for check-in}}.
explanation: ready for check-in means that the hotel room has been cleaned, inspected, and is fully prepared for the guest to enter and receive their key.
usage:
Pattern: ready for check-in
  • Is my room ready for check-in? Standard check-in is at 3 PM.
  • The front desk agent told me the room is not ready yet.
spanish: Tengo una reservación a nombre de Pérez, y me gustaría verificar si mi habitación está lista para registrarme.

text: I have a reservation under the name Colin, and I'd like to {{c1::check in}}.
explanation: To 'check in' at a hotel is the formal process of registering, showing ID, providing payment, and receiving room keys.
usage:
Pattern: check in / check-in desk
  • We can't check in until 3:00 PM.
  • Where is the check-in counter?
spanish: Tengo una reservación bajo el nombre Colin, y me gustaría registrarme.

text: Does the room rate {{c1::include breakfast}}, or is there an extra charge?
explanation: To 'include breakfast' means that breakfast is provided for free as part of the price paid for the hotel room (often called 'complimentary breakfast').
usage:
Pattern: include breakfast / complimentary breakfast
  • Does this booking include breakfast?
  • Our stay includes a free buffet breakfast.
spanish: ¿La tarifa de la habitación incluye desayuno, o hay un cargo extra?

text: Could we request a {{c1::late check-out}} for tomorrow at 2:00 PM?
explanation: A 'late check-out' is an arrangement allowing hotel guests to stay in their room past the standard checkout time (typically 11:00 AM or 12:00 PM).
usage:
Pattern: late check-out / request a late check-out
  • We requested a late check-out because our flight is at night.
  • The hotel charged us a fee for late check-out.
spanish: ¿Podríamos solicitar una salida tardía para mañana a las 2:00 PM?

text: Excuse me, does the hotel offer a {{c1::shuttle service}} to the airport?
explanation: A 'shuttle service' is a regular, complimentary transport service (usually a van or bus) provided by a hotel to move guests to and from specific locations like airports or tourist sights.
usage:
Pattern: shuttle service / airport shuttle
  • The hotel shuttle service runs every half hour.
  • We took the free shuttle service to the beach.
spanish: Disculpe, ¿el hotel ofrece servicio de transporte gratuito al aeropuerto?

text: My key card is not working; could you please {{c1::re-key it}} for me?
explanation: To 're-key' a card means to reprogram the electronic magnetic strip or RFID chip on a hotel key card so that it opens the guest's room door.
usage:
Pattern: re-key the card / reprogram the key
  • My card got deactivated near my phone, so they had to re-key it.
  • The receptionist re-keyed my card in seconds.
spanish: Mi tarjeta llave no funciona; ¿podría volver a programarla para mí?

text: Could you please send some extra towels and toiletries up to {{c1::our room}}?
explanation: To 'send up to a room' is the standard phrasal expression used by guests when requesting room service or housekeeping to deliver items directly to their hotel room.
usage:
Pattern: send [items] up to our room
  • Could you send a bottle of water up to room 405?
  • Housekeeping will send the pillows up to your room.
spanish: ¿Podría por favor enviar algunas toallas y artículos de tocador adicionales a nuestra habitación?

text: Is there a {{c1::safe deposit box}} in the room to store our valuables?
explanation: A 'safe deposit box' (or in-room safe) is a small, secure electronic metal box inside a hotel room closet where guests can store passports, cash, and jewelry.
usage:
Pattern: safe deposit box / room safe
  • I put my passport in the safe deposit box.
  • Make sure you lock the safe deposit box before leaving.
spanish: ¿Hay caja fuerte en la habitación para guardar nuestros objetos de valor?

text: I need to make a phone call, is there free {{c1::local calling}} from the room?
explanation: 'Local calling' refers to telephone calls made to numbers within the same city or local calling area, which hotels often offer for free.
usage:
Pattern: local calling / long-distance calls
  • Local calling is free, but long-distance calls will be charged to your room.
  • Does this phone support free local calling?
spanish: Necesito hacer una llamada telefónica, ¿hay llamadas locales gratuitas desde la habitación?

text: We'd like to {{c1::leave our bags}} at the front desk until our room is ready.
explanation: To 'leave bags' (or store luggage) means to temporarily place your suitcases in the hotel's secure holding room when arriving before check-in or departing after check-out.
usage:
Pattern: leave our bags / store our luggage
  • Can we leave our bags here while we explore the city?
  • You can leave your bags with the bellhop.
spanish: Nos gustaría dejar nuestras maletas en recepción hasta que nuestra habitación esté lista.

text: I'd like to request a room with a view of the ocean, if you have any {{c1::availability}}.
explanation: 'Availability' refers to the state of being vacant, unoccupied, or ready for booking (e.g. empty hotel rooms).
usage:
Pattern: room availability / check availability
  • Do you have any availability for this weekend?
  • Please check the room availability on the database.
spanish: Me gustaría solicitar una habitación con vista al océano, si tienen alguna disponibilidad.

text: Is there a gym or a fitness center inside the hotel that guests can use {{c1::free of charge}}?
explanation: 'Free of charge' is a professional phrase meaning without any payment, cost, or fee required.
usage:
Pattern: free of charge / complimentary
  • The hotel provides Wi-Fi free of charge.
  • Parking is available free of charge for guests.
spanish: ¿Hay un gimnasio o centro de acondicionamiento físico dentro del hotel que los huéspedes puedan usar de forma gratuita?

text: Excuse me, what time does the pool close, and do we need to bring our own {{c1::towels}}?
explanation: A 'towel' is a sheet of absorbent fabric or paper used for wiping or drying your body or a surface.
usage:
Pattern: bath towels / pool towels
  • Housekeeping left some fresh towels in the bathroom.
  • You can collect pool towels at the reception desk.
spanish: Disculpe, ¿a qué hora cierra la piscina, y necesitamos llevar nuestras propias toallas?

text: Could you arrange a wake-up call for tomorrow morning at {{c1::6:00 AM}}?
explanation: A 'wake-up call' is a telephone call arranged by a hotel guest with the front desk to wake them up at a specific time in the morning.
usage:
Pattern: wake-up call / request a wake-up call
  • I set a wake-up call for 5:00 AM to catch my early flight.
  • Did you receive your wake-up call?
spanish: ¿Podría programar una llamada de despertador para mañana a las 6:00 AM?

text: I need to print some flight tickets; is there a computer available in the {{c1::business center}}?
explanation: A 'business center' is a room in a hotel equipped with computers, printers, scanners, and internet access for guests to use.
usage:
Pattern: business center / office room
  • The business center is open 24 hours a day on the second floor.
  • I printed my boarding pass in the business center.
spanish: Necesito imprimir algunos boletos de avión; ¿hay una computadora disponible en el centro de negocios?

text: Is the tap water in the bathroom safe to drink, or should we use {{c1::bottled water}}?
explanation: 'Bottled water' is purified drinking water packaged in plastic or glass bottles, commonly provided in hotel rooms.
usage:
Pattern: bottled water / tap water
  • We always buy bottled water when traveling abroad.
  • The hotel provides two complimentary bottles of water daily.
spanish: ¿El agua del grifo en el baño es segura para beber, o deberíamos usar agua embotellada?

text: We need to check out tomorrow morning; can we pay the bill in cash or should we keep it on the {{c1::credit card}}?
explanation: A 'credit card' is a plastic card issued by a bank allowing the holder to purchase goods or services on credit.
usage:
Pattern: credit card / debit card
  • We charged our hotel stay to our corporate credit card.
  • Do you accept this credit card?
spanish: Necesitamos hacer el check-out mañana por la mañana; ¿podemos pagar la cuenta en efectivo o la dejamos en la tarjeta de crédito?

text: Could you call a taxi for us to take us to the train station after we {{c1::check out}}?
explanation: To 'check out' at a hotel is the formal process of leaving the room, settling the bill, and returning room keys.
usage:
Pattern: check out / check-out time
  • The checkout time is 11:00 AM.
  • We checked out of the hotel early in the morning.
spanish: ¿Podría pedir un taxi para nosotros que nos lleve a la estación de tren después de que hagamos el check-out?

text: Does this hotel provide laundry service, and how long does it take to get the clothes {{c1::returned}}?
explanation: To get clothes 'returned' is the standard terminology for receiving laundered or dry-cleaned garments back from the hotel service.
usage:
Pattern: returned laundry / dry cleaning delivery
  • If I drop off my suit now, when will it be returned?
  • Your laundry will be returned by tomorrow evening.
spanish: ¿Este hotel ofrece servicio de lavandería, y cuánto tiempo toma tener la ropa de vuelta?

text: Pardon me, where is the emergency exit located on this {{c1::floor}}?
explanation: A 'floor' (or level/story) refers to the entire level of a building, containing rooms and hallways on that horizontal plane.
usage:
Pattern: on this floor / ground floor / top floor
  • Our room is located on the fourth floor.
  • Housekeeping is currently cleaning the third floor.
spanish: Disculpe, ¿dónde se encuentra la salida de emergencia en este piso?

text: Can I get a cheeseburger and {{c1::a side of}} fries, please?
explanation: a side of is the standard, natural collocation to order an additional side portion of food alongside your main dish.
usage:
Pattern: a side of [food]
  • I'd like a steak with a side of mashed potatoes.
  • Can I get a side of ranch dressing?
spanish: ¿Me da una hamburguesa con queso y una porción de papas fritas, por favor?

text: We are ready to order: I'll have the steak, and can I get the dressing {{c1::on the side}}?
explanation: To get a sauce or dressing 'on the side' means it should be served in a small separate cup rather than poured directly over the food.
usage:
Pattern: on the side / sauce on the side
  • I'd like a salad with vinaigrette on the side.
  • Can I get the gravy on the side?
spanish: Estamos listos para pedir: Tomaré el filete, ¿y me puede dar el aderezo aparte/a un lado?

text: Excuse me, I'm allergic to nuts; does this dish {{c1::contain any nuts}}?
explanation: To 'contain nuts' is a crucial health expression used to check if tree nuts or peanuts are used in the ingredients or preparation of a food item.
usage:
Pattern: contain nuts / nut-free
  • Does the pesto sauce contain any nuts?
  • Please let the chef know that my food must not contain any nuts.
spanish: Disculpe, soy alérgico a las nueces; ¿este plato contiene nueces?

text: Can we get a {{c1::pitcher of tap water}} for the table, please?
explanation: A 'pitcher of tap water' is a large container of municipal drinking water provided for free in US/UK restaurants, as opposed to bottled water.
usage:
Pattern: pitcher of tap water / jug of water
  • Just a pitcher of tap water is fine, thank you.
  • We always order a pitcher of tap water.
spanish: ¿Nos puede traer una jarra de agua del grifo para la mesa, por favor?

text: I'd like my steak cooked {{c1::medium-rare}}, please.
explanation: 'Medium-rare' is the standard cooking level for meat where the center is warm and red, generally preferred by culinary experts for texture and flavor.
usage:
Pattern: medium-rare / rare / medium / well-done
  • I prefer my ribeye cooked medium-rare.
  • He ordered his burger well-done.
spanish: Me gustaría mi filete término medio (tres cuartos), por favor.

text: Excuse me, we've been waiting for our food for thirty minutes; could you {{c1::check on our order}}?
explanation: To 'check on an order' is a polite way to ask a server to go to the kitchen and find out why the food is delayed.
usage:
Pattern: check on our order / verify the status
  • Could you please check on our order? It's taking a bit long.
  • I'll go to the kitchen to check on your order right away.
spanish: Disculpe, hemos estado esperando nuestra comida por treinta minutos; ¿podría revisar nuestro pedido?

text: This isn't what I ordered; I asked for the grilled chicken, not the {{c1::fried chicken}}.
explanation: This is a polite yet direct way to notify a server that they have brought the wrong dish to your table.
usage:
Pattern: this isn't what I ordered / wrong dish
  • Excuse me, this isn't what I ordered; I wanted a salad.
  • I apologize, I'll take this back and bring you the correct plate.
spanish: Esto no es lo que pedí; pedí el pollo a la parrilla, no el pollo frito.

text: We are done eating, could you please {{c1::bring us the check}}?
explanation: To 'bring the check' (or bring the bill) is the standard request to pay for the meal at the end of dining.
usage:
Pattern: bring us the check / can we get the bill
  • Excuse me, can you bring us the check when you have a moment?
  • He asked the waiter to bring the check.
spanish: Hemos terminado de comer, ¿podría por favor traernos la cuenta?

text: Do you have any vegan options, or can this dish be {{c1::made vegan}}?
explanation: To make a dish 'vegan' means modifying the recipe to exclude all animal products (meat, dairy, eggs, honey).
usage:
Pattern: made vegan / vegan-friendly
  • Can this pasta be made vegan if you remove the cheese?
  • Is there a vegan alternative for this sauce?
spanish: ¿Tienen alguna opción vegana, o este plato se puede hacer vegano?

text: Can we split the bill {{c1::evenly between}} the three of us?
explanation: To 'split the bill evenly' means to divide the total cost of a restaurant meal into equal portions among the diners.
usage:
Pattern: split the bill / go Dutch
  • Let's split the bill evenly to keep things simple.
  • We decided to split the bill instead of paying separately.
spanish: ¿Podemos dividir la cuenta en partes iguales entre los tres?

text: Could we get a menu to look at the desserts, or do you have a {{c1::dessert tray}}?
explanation: A 'dessert tray' is a platter or cart displaying physical samples of available desserts, shown to diners at the end of a meal to encourage them to order.
usage:
Pattern: dessert tray / dessert menu
  • The waiter brought a dessert tray showing cheesecakes and chocolate tarts.
  • I was too full until I saw the dessert tray.
spanish: ¿Podríamos pedir un menú para ver los postres, o tiene una bandeja de postres?

text: Excuse me, I ordered my salad without onions, but this one has {{c1::onions on it}}.
explanation: This is a polite way to point out that a kitchen error occurred, specifically regarding a requested ingredient omission (onions).
usage:
Pattern: without [ingredient], but it has [ingredient] on it
  • I asked for no cheese, but this burger has cheese on it.
  • It has nuts on it; I cannot eat it.
spanish: Disculpe, pedí mi ensalada sin cebolla, pero esta tiene cebollas encima.

text: Is it possible to replace the french fries with a side salad instead, without any {{c1::extra charge}}?
explanation: An 'extra charge' (or surcharge) is an additional cost added to the bill for modifying a dish or requesting premium substitutions.
usage:
Pattern: extra charge / additional fee
  • Substituting almond milk has an extra charge of 50 cents.
  • There is no extra charge for extra ice.
spanish: ¿Es posible reemplazar las patatas fritas con una ensalada de acompañamiento, sin ningún cargo adicional?

text: We'd like to order a bottle of red wine; which one do you recommend to pair with the {{c1::steak}}?
explanation: 'Steak' is a high-quality slice of beef (like ribeye or sirloin), commonly paired with red wine (like Cabernet Sauvignon) in fine dining.
usage:
Pattern: steak pair with [wine] / T-bone steak
  • We ordered a medium-rare steak with a side of asparagus.
  • Does this steak come with mushroom sauce?
spanish: Nos gustaría pedir una botella de vino tinto; ¿cuál nos recomienda para maridar con el filete?

text: Could we get some extra napkins and a clean fork for the table, please? One of ours {{c1::fell on the floor}}.
explanation: To 'fall on the floor' (or drop on the floor) is the standard phrase used to explain why you need a replacement utensil from the server.
usage:
Pattern: fell on the floor / dropped on the ground
  • I need a clean knife; this one fell on the floor.
  • The glass fell on the floor and shattered.
spanish: ¿Podríamos pedir algunas servilletas adicionales y un tenedor limpio para la mesa, por favor? Uno de los nuestros se cayó al suelo.

text: The soup is a bit cold, could you please have the kitchen {{c1::reheat it}} for me?
explanation: To 'reheat' food means to warm it up again, commonly requested in restaurants if a dish arrives lukewarm.
usage:
Pattern: reheat the food / warm it back up
  • Could you reheat this coffee? It has gone completely cold.
  • I'll ask the chef to reheat your soup right away.
spanish: La sopa está un poco fría, ¿podría por favor hacer que la cocina la recaliente por mí?

text: Can we get these leftovers packed in a box to take home, or do you have {{c1::to-go containers}}?
explanation: A 'to-go container' (or doggy bag) is a disposable plastic or paper box provided by a restaurant so guests can carry their leftovers home.
usage:
Pattern: to-go containers / doggy bag / takeout box
  • We couldn't finish our pizzas, so we asked for to-go containers.
  • Do you charge for to-go containers?
spanish: ¿Podemos llevarnos lo que sobró empacado en una caja a casa, o tienen envases para llevar?

text: Is service included in the bill, or is the tip {{c1::left to our discretion}}?
explanation: 'Left to our discretion' is a formal phrase meaning that a choice or amount (like a gratuity/tip) is not mandatory and is decided entirely by the customer.
usage:
Pattern: left to [someone's] discretion / optional choice
  • The tip is left to the client's discretion.
  • The dress code is left to the manager's discretion.
spanish: ¿El servicio está incluido en la cuenta, o la propina queda a nuestra discreción?

text: Do you accept credit cards, or is this restaurant {{c1::cash-only}}?
explanation: 'Cash-only' describes a business that only accepts physical money (banknotes/coins) for payment, refusing credit cards or digital transfers.
usage:
Pattern: cash-only business / cash payment
  • This coffee shop is cash-only, but there is an ATM nearby.
  • I couldn't buy lunch because the food truck was cash-only.
spanish: ¿Aceptan tarjetas de crédito, o este restaurante es de solo efectivo?

text: Can we book a table for six people for this Friday night at {{c1::8:00 PM}}?
explanation: To 'book a table' means to make a reservation in advance at a restaurant.
usage:
Pattern: book a table / make a reservation
  • I need to book a table for our anniversary dinner.
  • It is hard to book a table at this restaurant on weekends.
spanish: ¿Podemos reservar una mesa para seis personas para este viernes por la noche a las 8:00 PM?

text: Where can I find the {{c1::dairy aisle}}?
explanation: An aisle is a passage between rows of shelves in a supermarket. dairy refers to milk-based products (cheese, yogurt, butter).
usage:
Pattern: [category] aisle (e.g., baking aisle, produce aisle, frozen food aisle)
  • The sugar is in the baking aisle.
  • Where is the produce aisle? I need some fresh apples.
spanish: ¿Dónde puedo encontrar el pasillo de lácteos?

text: Are these apples {{c1::on sale}} this week?
explanation: on sale means available at a reduced price (discounted). *Do not confuse with 'for sale', which just means available to buy.
usage:
Pattern: on sale (discounted) vs for sale (available to buy)
  • These shoes are on sale for 20% off.
  • Is that classic car for sale or is it just on display?
spanish: ¿Están estas manzanas en oferta esta semana?

text: Is there a limit on how many items I can bring to the {{c1::express lane}}?
explanation: An express lane is a checkout counter reserved for customers buying only a few items (e.g. 10 or less) to speed up service.
usage:
Pattern: express lane (typically labeled '10 items or less' or '15 items or fewer')
  • I only have 3 items, so I will use the express lane.
  • You can't go there; the express lane is for quick checkouts.
spanish: ¿Hay un límite de cuántos artículos puedo llevar a la caja rápida?

text: At the customer service counter, the customer requested: "I'd like a {{c1::full refund}} for this tablet because it has a {{c2::faulty charging port}}."
explanation: Refund is the return of payment. **Faulty** means defective or not working correctly.
usage:
Pattern: request a refund for [item] / faulty [part]
  • I need a full refund because the screen is cracked.
  • This laptop has a faulty battery that doesn't charge.
spanish: En el mostrador de servicio al cliente, el cliente solicitó: "Me gustaría un reembolso completo por esta tableta porque tiene un puerto de carga defectuoso".

text: At the flea market, he bargained: "Is that your {{c1::best price}}? If you {{c2::throw in the vintage watch}}, we have a deal."
explanation: Best price is the lowest price a seller is willing to accept. **Throw in** is a phrasal verb meaning to include something extra for free.
usage:
Pattern: throw in [item] / have a deal
  • If you throw in free shipping, I'll buy it.
  • We have a deal if you lower the price by ten dollars.
spanish: En el mercado de pulgas, regateó: "¿Es ese tu mejor precio? Si incluyes el reloj vintage, tenemos un trato".

text: When comparing laptops, he analyzed: "There are clear {{c1::trade-offs}} in portability, but the developer version gives you the most {{c2::bang for the buck}}."
explanation: Trade-offs are compromises made when choosing between two conflicting features. **Bang for the buck** is an idiom meaning value for money spent.
usage:
Pattern: trade-offs between [feature] and [feature] / get the most bang for the buck
  • There are always trade-offs between battery life and processing power.
  • This budget phone offers the best bang for the buck.
spanish: Al comparar computadoras portátiles, analizó: "Hay compensaciones claras en la portabilidad, pero la versión para desarrolladores te ofrece la mejor relación calidad-precio".

text: On the phone with the bank, she stated: "I need to {{c1::dispute a double charge}} that appeared on my recent {{c2::billing cycle statement}}."
explanation: Dispute a charge is the formal request to investigate an incorrect transaction. **Billing cycle statement** is the monthly bank report.
usage:
Pattern: dispute a charge on [account/statement]
  • You should dispute the charge immediately with your credit card company.
  • I check my billing cycle statement every month for errors.
spanish: Por teléfono con el banco, ella declaró: "Necesito impugnar un cargo doble que apareció en el estado de cuenta de mi ciclo de facturación reciente".

text: Before subscribing, he asked: "Is there an {{c1::auto-renewal policy}}? I want to make sure I read the {{c2::fine print}} to avoid hidden fees."
explanation: Auto-renewal is the automatic extension of a subscription. **Fine print** refers to the small text in a contract containing crucial restrictions and rules.
usage:
Pattern: auto-renewal policy / read the fine print
  • Always read the fine print before signing a software license.
  • The auto-renewal policy can be turned off in your account settings.
spanish: Antes de suscribirse, preguntó: "¿Existe una política de renovación automática? Quiero asegurarme de leer la letra pequeña para evitar cargos ocultos".

text: Excuse me, in which {{c1::aisle}} can I find the olive oil?
explanation: An 'aisle' is a long, narrow passage between shelves of goods in a supermarket or grocery store (the 's' is silent, pronounced /aɪl/).
usage:
Pattern: aisle [number] / product aisle
  • The baking ingredients are in aisle 4.
  • Excuse me, which aisle is the cereal in?
spanish: Disculpe, ¿en qué pasillo puedo encontrar el aceite de oliva?

text: I'll pay in cash, and I don't need a {{c1::receipt}}, thank you.
explanation: A 'receipt' is a printed slip of paper given to customers to prove that a financial transaction took place (the 'p' is silent, pronounced /rɪˈsiːt/).
usage:
Pattern: sales receipt / cash receipt
  • Always keep your receipt in case you need to return the item.
  • Do you want your receipt in the bag?
spanish: Pagaré en efectivo, y no necesito recibo, gracias.

text: Could you please help me find the dairy section? I need to buy some yogurt and {{c1::sour cream}}.
explanation: 'Sour cream' is cream soured by lactic acid bacteria, commonly used in cooking or as a condiment/sauce.
usage:
Pattern: sour cream / whipping cream
  • I put a dollop of sour cream on my baked potato.
  • We need sour cream to make the taco dip.
spanish: ¿Podrías por favor ayudarme a encontrar la sección de lácteos? Necesito comprar algo de yogur y crema agria.

text: Is there a discount on these apples if I buy them in bulk, or is the price {{c1::fixed per pound}}?
explanation: A price that is 'fixed per pound' (or fixed per kilo) means it does not decrease when buying larger quantities; it scales linearly with weight.
usage:
Pattern: fixed per [unit] / bulk discount
  • The organic grapes are fixed per pound.
  • How much does this cost? It is fixed per kilogram.
spanish: ¿Hay algún descuento en estas manzanas si las compro al por mayor, o el precio es fijo por libra?

text: Excuse me, where are the shopping carts located? I only see {{c1::baskets}} near the entrance.
explanation: A 'basket' is a small hand-held container with a handle, used by shoppers carrying only a few items in a supermarket, as opposed to a rolling cart.
usage:
Pattern: shopping baskets / wire baskets
  • I grabbed a shopping basket because I only needed bread and eggs.
  • Please return the baskets near the checkout counter.
spanish: Disculpe, ¿dónde están ubicados los carritos de compra? Solo veo canastas cerca de la entrada.

text: I need to buy some fresh fish, but the seafood counter is {{c1::unattended}} right now.
explanation: 'Unattended' means not supervised, served, or looked after by any staff member.
usage:
Pattern: unattended desk / unattended counter
  • Do not leave your bags unattended at the airport.
  • The customer service desk was temporarily unattended.
spanish: Necesito comprar pescado fresco, pero el mostrador de mariscos está desatendido en este momento.

text: Are these vegetables organic, or are they grown using {{c1::conventional methods}}?
explanation: 'Conventional methods' in agriculture refer to standard farming practices that may involve synthetic fertilizers, pesticides, and herbicides, distinct from organic farming.
usage:
Pattern: conventional farming / conventional methods
  • Conventional crops are usually cheaper than organic ones.
  • This farm transitioned from conventional methods to organic agriculture.
spanish: ¿Estas verduras son orgánicas, o son cultivadas usando métodos convencionales?

text: Excuse me, is this item on sale? The price tag shows a {{c1::discount code}} but I'm not sure.
explanation: A 'discount code' is a series of letters or numbers that customers can enter at checkout (or scanning tags) to receive a price reduction.
usage:
Pattern: discount code / promotional coupon
  • Enter the discount code at checkout to save 10%.
  • The cashier scanned the discount code on my receipt.
spanish: Disculpe, ¿este artículo está en oferta? La etiqueta del precio muestra un código de descuento pero no estoy seguro.

text: I have some coupons; can I scan them at the self-checkout register, or should I go to a {{c1::cashier}}?
explanation: A 'cashier' is the store employee who operates the cash register, processes customer payments, and bags purchases.
usage:
Pattern: cashier counter / check out with cashier
  • The cashier scanned my items and handed me the receipt.
  • I prefer checking out with a cashier instead of using the machine.
spanish: Tengo algunos cupones; ¿puedo escanearlos en la caja de autoservicio, o debería ir con un cajero?

text: I'd like to buy some fresh bread; has the bakery section finished baking the {{c1::baguettes}}?
explanation: A 'baguette' is a long, narrow loaf of French bread, known for its crispy crust and soft interior.
usage:
Pattern: fresh baguettes / bakery section
  • We bought two fresh baguettes for the picnic.
  • The baguettes are baked fresh every morning.
spanish: Me gustaría comprar pan fresco; ¿la sección de panadería ha terminado de hornear las baguettes?

text: Do you charge extra for paper bags, or should we bring our own {{c1::reusable bags}}?
explanation: A 'reusable bag' is a shopping bag made of durable fabric or plastic, designed to be used multiple times to reduce environmental waste.
usage:
Pattern: reusable bags / canvas shopping bag
  • Keep some reusable bags in your car trunk for grocery shopping.
  • Using reusable bags helps reduce plastic pollution.
spanish: ¿Cobran extra por las bolsas de papel, o deberíamos traer nuestras propias bolsas reutilizables?

text: Please place the fragile items, like eggs and tomatoes, on {{c1::top of the bag}}.
explanation: 'On top of the bag' is a spatial instruction telling a packer or cashier to place delicate items at the very top of a grocery bag so they are not crushed by heavier items underneath.
usage:
Pattern: top of the bag / bottom of the bag
  • Do not put the heavy cans on top of the bread.
  • Keep the eggs at the top of the bag.
spanish: Por favor, coloque los artículos frágiles, como huevos y tomates, arriba de la bolsa.

text: Hi, I have a reservation and I’d like to {{c1::check in}}.
explanation: This is a standard hotel phrase. In natural speech, speakers often use 'I'd like to' to sound polite and smooth.
usage:
Pattern: check in / check-in desk
  • We’d like to check in early.
  • Where is the check-in desk?
spanish: Hola, tengo una reservación y me gustaría registrarme.

text: Can you take me to the {{c1::airport}} as quickly as possible?
explanation: A natural travel sentence for a taxi or rideshare. The phrase sounds practical and casual.
usage:
Pattern: take me to the airport
  • Can you take me to the station?
  • Take me to the hotel, please.
spanish: ¿Puede llevarme al aeropuerto lo más rápido posible?

text: Is there a {{c1::direct flight}} to Mexico City?
explanation: Direct flight is a common phrase used when planning or confirming a trip.
usage:
Pattern: direct flight / nonstop flight
  • Is there a nonstop flight?
  • How long is the direct flight?
spanish: ¿Hay un vuelo directo a la Ciudad de México?

text: I think our flight is going to be {{c1::delayed}}.
explanation: Delayed is an extremely useful word in travel conversations and is often said in a calm, practical tone.
usage:
Pattern: be delayed / running late
  • The train is delayed.
  • We’re running late.
spanish: Creo que nuestro vuelo se va a retrasar.

text: Could you help me with my {{c1::luggage}}?
explanation: Luggage is the general word for suitcases and bags, especially in travel situations.
usage:
Pattern: carry-on luggage / check-in luggage
  • I need to check in my luggage.
  • My carry-on is too heavy.
spanish: ¿Podría ayudarme con mi equipaje?

scenario: Travel English: Room with a View 🌊
text: Do you have any rooms with a {{c1::view}}?
explanation: A very natural phrase used at hotels when asking for a better room or a specific environment.
usage:
Pattern: room with a view / ocean view
  • Do you have a room with a view?
  • We’d like an ocean view if possible.
spanish: ¿Tienen habitaciones con vista?

text: Can we {{c1::leave our bags}} here until the room is ready?
explanation: This is common at hotels when you arrive early and need to store your luggage.
usage:
Pattern: leave bags / store luggage
  • Can we store our luggage here?
  • We’ll leave our bags at reception.
spanish: ¿Podemos dejar nuestras maletas aquí hasta que la habitación esté lista?

text: The traffic is a {{c1::nightmare}} today.
explanation: Nightmare is a strong colloquial expression to say something is very bad or frustrating.
usage:
Pattern: traffic is a nightmare / the queue was a nightmare
  • The traffic was a nightmare this morning.
  • The line at the airport was a nightmare.
spanish: El tráfico es una pesadilla hoy.

text: I appreciate your perspective, so let's find a {{c1::middle ground}} that addresses both of our concerns.
explanation: middle ground is a common idiom meaning a compromise or a set of mutual concessions between two opposing viewpoints.
usage:
Pattern: find a middle ground / reach a middle ground
  • After hours of debate, we finally found a middle ground.
  • It is difficult to reach a middle ground on this budget issue.
spanish: Aprecio tu perspectiva, así que busquemos un punto medio que aborde ambas preocupaciones.

text: I understand your frustration, let's focus on finding a {{c1::middle ground}} that works for both teams.
explanation: A 'middle ground' is a compromise or position of agreement between two opposing viewpoints or parties.
usage:
Pattern: find a middle ground / compromise
  • Negotiations succeeded once both sides found a middle ground.
  • We need to establish a middle ground regarding work hours.
spanish: Entiendo su frustración, enfoquémonos en encontrar un punto medio/acuerdo que funcione para ambos equipos.

text: Let's schedule a call to {{c1::iron out}} these misunderstandings before the client meeting.
explanation: To 'iron out' is a phrasal verb meaning to resolve, smooth over, or eliminate minor problems or differences in views.
usage:
Pattern: iron out [problems / details / differences]
  • We need to iron out the details of the contract.
  • They met to iron out their political differences.
spanish: Agendemos una llamada para resolver/limar estas asperezas antes de la reunión con el cliente.

text: I want to make sure I fully grasp your point; could you please {{c1::elaborate on}} your concerns?
explanation: To 'elaborate on' means to add more details, explanation, or information to a statement that was previously brief.
usage:
Pattern: elaborate on [point / concerns / strategy]
  • Could you elaborate on how this database schema works?
  • The manager elaborated on the restructuring plan.
spanish: Quiero asegurarme de entender completamente su punto; ¿podría por favor detallar/explayarse sobre sus preocupaciones?

text: Let's keep this discussion objective and avoid {{c1::pointing fingers}} at individuals.
explanation: To 'point fingers' is an idiomatic expression meaning to accuse or place blame on someone else for a failure or problem.
usage:
Pattern: point fingers / play the blame game
  • Instead of pointing fingers, let's focus on fixing the bug.
  • She is always quick to point fingers when things go wrong.
spanish: Mantengamos esta discusión objetiva y evitemos buscar culpables/echarle la culpa a individuos.

text: I appreciate you bringing this up; let's work together to {{c1::address the root cause}} of this issue.
explanation: To 'address the root cause' means to identify and fix the underlying, fundamental reason why a problem occurred, rather than just treating symptoms.
usage:
Pattern: address the root cause / resolve root issue
  • Rebooting the server only patches the symptom; we must address the root cause.
  • Our investigation aimed to address the root cause of the data breach.
spanish: Le agradezco que mencione esto; trabajemos juntos para abordar la causa raíz de este problema.

text: We seem to have reached a {{c1::deadlock}}; let's bring in a neutral third party to mediate.
explanation: A 'deadlock' (or impasse) is a situation in a negotiation where no progress is possible because neither party is willing to compromise.
usage:
Pattern: reach a deadlock / break a deadlock
  • The contract talks ended in a complete deadlock.
  • We need a mediator to help us break the deadlock.
spanish: Parece que hemos llegado a un punto muerto/callejón sin salida; involucremos a un tercero neutral para mediar.

text: I agree that we need to act fast, but we cannot afford to {{c1::cut corners}} on safety.
explanation: To 'cut corners' is an idiom meaning to do something in the easiest, cheapest, or fastest way, often sacrificing quality or safety standards.
usage:
Pattern: cut corners / skimp on quality
  • The builder cut corners and used cheap materials.
  • Never cut corners on database security audits.
spanish: Estoy de acuerdo en que debemos actuar rápido, pero no podemos permitirnos tomar atajos/escatimar en seguridad.

text: Let's take a step back and look at the {{c1::bigger picture}} before making a decision.
explanation: The 'bigger picture' refers to the overall context, long-term goals, or broad view of a situation, rather than small details.
usage:
Pattern: look at the bigger picture / overview
  • Don't get bogged down in detail; look at the bigger picture.
  • A manager must always focus on the bigger picture.
spanish: Demos un paso atrás y miremos el panorama completo/la perspectiva general antes de tomar una decisión.

text: I apologize for the oversight; I will take full responsibility and {{c1::make it right}}.
explanation: To 'make it right' means to correct a mistake, repair a bad situation, or compensate someone for an error.
usage:
Pattern: make it right / repair the damage
  • We sent the client a refund to make it right.
  • If you make a mistake, do your best to make it right.
spanish: Pido disculpas por el descuido; asumiré toda la responsabilidad y lo corregiré/enmendaré.

text: Instead of focusing on past mistakes, let's look forward and find a constructive way to {{c1::move past this}}.
explanation: To 'move past' a problem means to accept what happened, stop arguing about it, and focus your energy on future progress.
usage:
Pattern: move past this / overcome conflict
  • We had differences, but we managed to move past them.
  • Let's move past this misunderstanding and continue working.
spanish: En lugar de enfocarnos en los errores pasados, miremos hacia adelante y encontremos una manera constructiva de superar esto.

text: I want to apologize if my comments came across as harsh; that was not my {{c1::intention}}.
explanation: 'Intention' refers to a person's aim, goal, or purpose behind an action or statement.
usage:
Pattern: have no intention / standard intention
  • It was not my intention to offend anyone.
  • We must clarify our intentions before signing the contract.
spanish: Quiero disculparme si mis comentarios parecieron duros; esa no era mi intención.

text: Let's schedule a facilitated workshop to resolve the friction between the development and product {{c1::teams}}.
explanation: In corporate settings, 'friction' or conflict commonly occurs between cross-functional teams (e.g. devs and product managers). Decoupling their workflows helps resolve this.
usage:
Pattern: friction between [teams] / resolve friction
  • We resolved the friction between dev and QA by aligning our goals.
  • Department silos often cause team friction.
spanish: Agendemos un taller facilitado para resolver la fricción entre los equipos de desarrollo y de producto.

text: We need to establish clear boundaries to prevent tasks from overlapping and causing future {{c1::friction}}.
explanation: 'Friction' refers to disagreement, conflict, or clash of wills between individuals or groups.
usage:
Pattern: cause friction / reduce friction
  • Ambiguity in roles can cause friction in the workspace.
  • Clear boundaries help reduce team friction.
spanish: Necesitamos establecer límites claros para evitar que las tareas se traslapen y causen fricciones futuras.

text: I agree that we have different approaches, but we must find a way to compromise and {{c1::collaborate effectively}}.
explanation: To 'collaborate effectively' means to work together productively, sharing goals and communicating openly to achieve a task successfully.
usage:
Pattern: collaborate effectively / team collaboration
  • Our team uses Slack to collaborate effectively from different locations.
  • We must collaborate effectively to launch on schedule.
spanish: Estoy de acuerdo en que tenemos enfoques diferentes, pero debemos encontrar una forma de llegar a un acuerdo y colaborar de manera efectiva.

text: Let's document the agreed-upon resolutions so we can refer back to them if issues {{c1::resurface}}.
explanation: To 'resurface' means to arise or become active again after a period of being hidden, resolved, or inactive.
usage:
Pattern: issues resurface / problems reappear
  • The database bugs resurfaced after the patch deployment.
  • We must act before past conflicts resurface.
spanish: Documentemos las resoluciones acordadas para que podamos consultarlas si los problemas resurgen/vuelven a aparecer.

text: I understand you have concerns about the new policy; let's discuss them openly in a {{c1::safe space}}.
explanation: A 'safe space' is a supportive, non-judgmental environment where people can speak openly about concerns or feelings without fear of retaliation.
usage:
Pattern: safe space / supportive environment
  • Our one-on-one calls are a safe space to share feedback.
  • We created a safe space for employees to report harassment.
spanish: Entiendo que tenga inquietudes sobre la nueva política; discutámoslas abiertamente en un espacio seguro.

text: We need to ensure that everyone feels heard and respected during this restructuring {{c1::process}}.
explanation: A 'restructuring process' involves reorganizing a company's hierarchy, teams, or operational flows, which often causes anxiety and conflict among staff.
usage:
Pattern: restructuring process / organizational change
  • The restructuring process led to several department merges.
  • We must communicate clearly during the restructuring process.
spanish: Necesitamos asegurarnos de que todos se sientan escuchados y respetados durante este proceso de reestructuración.

text: Instead of assuming bad intentions, let's give our teammates the {{c1::benefit of the doubt}}.
explanation: To give someone the 'benefit of the doubt' means to believe their statements or assume their actions are benign, even if you are not completely sure.
usage:
Pattern: give the benefit of the doubt
  • He was late, but I gave him the benefit of the doubt because of traffic.
  • Always give colleagues the benefit of the doubt before raising issues.
spanish: En lugar de asumir malas intenciones, demos a nuestros compañeros de trabajo el beneficio de la duda.

text: If we cannot reach an agreement, we will have to escalate this decision to the steering {{c1::committee}}.
explanation: A 'steering committee' is a advisory board of high-level stakeholders that guides a project and resolves high-level conflicts or deadlocks.
usage:
Pattern: steering committee / governing board
  • The steering committee rejected the proposed budget increase.
  • Escalate the contract dispute to the steering committee.
spanish: Si no podemos llegar a un acuerdo, tendremos que escalar esta decisión al comité de dirección.

text: We need to {{c1::double down on}} our core product line to capture market share this quarter.
explanation: double down on means to significantly strengthen your commitment, focus, or investment in a strategy, instead of spreading resources too thin.
usage:
Pattern: double down on [focus/strategy/effort]
  • We decided to double down on cloud security features.
  • The team doubled down on automated testing to fix regression bugs.
spanish: Necesitamos redoblar esfuerzos en nuestra línea de productos principal para capturar participación de mercado este trimestre.

text: Given our current budget parameters, we must {{c1::trim the fat}} in our operating expenses.
explanation: trim the fat is a common business idiom meaning to eliminate waste, unnecessary expenses, or redundancies to make operations leaner.
usage:
Pattern: trim the fat (idiom)
  • To achieve profitability, the division needs to trim the fat.
  • Trimming the fat in marketing helped us fund research and development.
spanish: Dados nuestros parámetros presupuestarios actuales, debemos recortar los gastos innecesarios en nuestros gastos operativos.

text: We need to align our department goals to support the company's {{c1::long-term roadmap}}.
explanation: A 'long-term roadmap' is a strategic plan that outlines the main goals and milestones of a company over a long period (e.g. 3 to 5 years).
usage:
Pattern: long-term roadmap / strategic vision
  • Our product roadmap spans over the next three years.
  • The director presented the long-term roadmap to the division.
spanish: Necesitamos alinear las metas de nuestro departamento para apoyar la hoja de ruta a largo plazo de la compañía.

text: My role is to steer the strategic direction and {{c1::empower our managers}} to execute it.
explanation: To 'empower managers' means giving them the authority, resources, and trust to make decisions and lead their teams independently.
usage:
Pattern: empower managers / delegate authority
  • Good directors empower managers rather than micromanage.
  • We must empower our front-line managers to resolve customer issues.
spanish: Mi rol es guiar la dirección estratégica y capacitar/dar autonomía a nuestros gerentes para ejecutarla.

text: We must ensure we are allocating our resources efficiently to {{c1::maximize ROI}}.
explanation: To 'maximize ROI' (Return on Investment) means to ensure that business spending yields the highest possible profit or strategic benefit.
usage:
Pattern: maximize ROI / boost returns
  • Our marketing budget is allocated to channels that maximize ROI.
  • The team restructured the project to maximize ROI.
spanish: Debemos asegurarnos de asignar nuestros recursos eficientemente para maximizar el retorno de la inversión.

text: I need to report our quarterly performance metrics directly to the {{c1::board of directors}}.
explanation: The 'board of directors' is the governing body of a corporation elected by shareholders to oversee management and protect their interests.
usage:
Pattern: board of directors / board members
  • The board of directors approved the acquisition.
  • He serves on the board of directors for three companies.
spanish: Necesito informar nuestras métricas de rendimiento trimestrales directamente a la junta directiva.

text: We are restructuring the department to improve cross-functional collaboration and {{c1::eliminate silos}}.
explanation: To 'eliminate silos' means to break down barriers between departments that prevent open communication, sharing of data, or collaborative work.
usage:
Pattern: eliminate silos / break down walls
  • A key goal of Agile is to eliminate silos between devs and QA.
  • We must eliminate departmental silos to speed up production.
spanish: Estamos reestructurando el departamento para mejorar la colaboración interfuncional y eliminar los silos/departamentos aislados.

text: We must foster a culture of innovation where calculated risk-taking is {{c1::encouraged rather than penalized}}.
explanation: This phrase describes a strategic environment where employees are motivated to try new ideas, knowing that mistakes in pursuit of innovation will not lead to negative career consequences.
usage:
Pattern: [something] is encouraged rather than penalized
  • Creative problem solving should be encouraged rather than penalized.
  • Taking initiative is encouraged rather than penalized in this team.
spanish: Debemos fomentar una cultura de innovación donde la toma de riesgos calculados sea alentada en lugar de penalizada.

text: I will represent our division at the upcoming global leadership summit to {{c1::showcase our achievements}}.
explanation: To 'showcase achievements' means to display or present positive results, milestones, or accomplishments to others in a prominent way.
usage:
Pattern: showcase achievements / highlight success
  • The annual report is an opportunity to showcase our achievements.
  • We built a dashboard to showcase team achievements to executives.
spanish: Representaré a nuestra división en la próxima cumbre global de liderazgo para exhibir/presentar nuestros logros.

text: We need to conduct a thorough risk assessment before entering this {{c1::joint venture}}.
explanation: A 'joint venture' is a business arrangement in which two or more parties agree to pool their resources for the purpose of accomplishing a specific task.
usage:
Pattern: joint venture / strategic partnership
  • The two auto giants formed a joint venture to build electric cars.
  • The project is a joint venture between public and private firms.
spanish: Necesitamos realizar una evaluación de riesgos exhaustiva antes de entrar en esta empresa conjunta/alianza comercial.

text: We are expanding our market presence globally and need to establish new offices in the {{c1::APAC region}}.
explanation: 'APAC' stands for Asia-Pacific, a common geographical business acronym representing countries in East Asia, South Asia, Southeast Asia, and Oceania.
usage:
Pattern: APAC region / EMEA region / LATAM region
  • Our APAC region division achieved the highest growth this year.
  • She was appointed as the director for the APAC region.
spanish: Estamos expandiendo nuestra presencia de mercado globalmente y necesitamos establecer nuevas oficinas en la región APAC.

text: Our priority is to ensure regulatory compliance and manage our corporate governance {{c1::standards}}.
explanation: 'Corporate governance standards' are the system of rules, practices, and processes by which a firm is directed and controlled, balancing stakeholder interests.
usage:
Pattern: governance standards / regulatory compliance
  • Directors are responsible for maintaining high corporate governance standards.
  • The audit evaluated our compliance with governance standards.
spanish: Nuestra prioridad es asegurar el cumplimiento regulatorio y gestionar nuestros estándares de gobierno corporativo.

text: We need to evaluate the feasibility of this project before committing a large portion of our {{c1::capital expenditure}}.
explanation: 'Capital expenditure' (CapEx) represents funds used by a company to acquire, upgrade, and maintain physical assets such as property, buildings, or technology.
usage:
Pattern: capital expenditure / CapEx / operational expenditure
  • The board approved a capital expenditure budget of $5M for server upgrades.
  • Our capital expenditure decreased this fiscal year.
spanish: Necesitamos evaluar la viabilidad de este proyecto antes de comprometer una gran parte de nuestro gasto de capital.

text: My goal is to cultivate a diverse leadership pipeline to sustain our company's long-term {{c1::growth}}.
explanation: A 'leadership pipeline' is the systematic process of identifying and developing high-potential employees to fill future leadership and management roles.
usage:
Pattern: leadership pipeline / talent pool
  • We launched a mentorship program to strengthen our leadership pipeline.
  • Our leadership pipeline was depleted after the layoffs.
spanish: Mi objetivo es cultivar una cantera de liderazgo diversa para sostener el crecimiento a largo plazo de nuestra compañía.

text: We are partnering with a top consultancy firm to streamline our business operations and reduce {{c1::overhead costs}}.
explanation: 'Overhead costs' (or overheads) are the ongoing business expenses that cannot be directly attributed to creating a product or service (e.g. rent, utilities, insurance).
usage:
Pattern: overhead costs / reduce overheads
  • We reduced overhead costs by transitioning to a remote work model.
  • Rent and insurance are common examples of overhead costs.
spanish: Nos estamos asociando con una firma de consultoría líder para agilizar nuestras operaciones comerciales y reducir los costos generales/de operación.

text: We must review the market trends and adjust our pricing strategy to remain {{c1::competitive}}.
explanation: To remain 'competitive' means maintaining the capability to sell products or services successfully relative to rivals in terms of quality, price, or value.
usage:
Pattern: remain competitive / competitive advantage
  • We must innovate continuously to remain competitive in the tech industry.
  • Lowering costs is essential to remain competitive.
spanish: Debemos revisar las tendencias del mercado y ajustar nuestra estrategia de precios para seguir siendo competitivos.

text: I will present our new sustainability initiatives at the upcoming annual shareholder {{c1::meeting}}.
explanation: A 'shareholder meeting' (or general assembly) is a scheduled event where corporate directors report performance, discuss strategy, and vote on resolutions with company shareholders.
usage:
Pattern: annual shareholder meeting / general assembly
  • Shareholders voted on the new board members during the annual meeting.
  • The director faced tough questions at the shareholder meeting.
spanish: Presentaré nuestras nuevas iniciativas de sustentabilidad en la próxima reunión/asamblea anual de accionistas.

text: We need to perform a SWOT analysis to identify our strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and {{c1::threats}}.
explanation: A 'SWOT analysis' is a strategic planning framework used to identify Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, and Threats related to business competition or project planning.
usage:
Pattern: SWOT analysis / strategic assessment
  • Our SWOT analysis revealed that competitor pricing is our main threat.
  • Conduct a SWOT analysis before entering the new market.
spanish: Necesitamos realizar un análisis FODA para identificar nuestras fortalezas, debilidades, oportunidades y amenazas.

text: Our division has exceeded its targets, and we will allocate additional budget for research and {{c1::development}}.
explanation: 'Research and Development' (R&D) refers to the investigative activities a business conducts to innovate, develop, and introduce new products and procedures.
usage:
Pattern: research and development / R&D budget
  • We invest 10% of our revenue back into research and development.
  • The R&D team developed a new speech recognition algorithm.
spanish: Nuestra división ha superado sus metas, y asignaremos presupuesto adicional para investigación y desarrollo.

text: We need to sign a non-disclosure agreement (NDA) before sharing our proprietary technologies with the {{c1::partner}}.
explanation: A 'non-disclosure agreement' (NDA) is a legal contract governing the sharing of confidential information, prohibiting the recipient from leaking it to rivals or the public.
usage:
Pattern: non-disclosure agreement / NDA signature
  • Make sure the client signs the non-disclosure agreement.
  • We cannot share source code without a signed NDA.
spanish: Necesitamos firmar un acuerdo de confidencialidad (NDA) antes de compartir nuestras tecnologías patentadas con el socio.

text: I want to ensure we are aligned, so I'd like to {{c1::address a pattern}} I've noticed in your recent deliverables.
explanation: address a pattern is a polite, constructive way to bring up a recurring issue in performance without making the employee feel immediately attacked.
usage:
Pattern: address a pattern of [behavior]
  • We need to address a pattern of late arrivals in morning standups.
  • Thank you for addressing a pattern that was affecting our release schedule.
spanish: Quiero asegurarme de que estemos alineados, por lo que me gustaría abordar un patrón que he notado en tus entregables recientes.

text: According to Kim Scott's Radical Candor framework, effective leadership requires balancing the two dimensions of {{c1::Care Personally}} (Cuidar Personalmente) and {{c2::Challenge Directly}} (Desafiar Directamente).
explanation: Failing to challenge directly while caring personally leads to 'Empatía Ruinosa' (Ruinous Empathy), which prevents growth. Challenging directly without caring leads to 'Agresión Ofensiva' (Obnoxious Aggression).
usage: Radical Candor is the 'sweet spot' where you care enough about someone to tell them the uncomfortable truth to help them grow.
spanish: Según el marco de Sinceridad Radical de Kim Scott, el liderazgo efectivo requiere equilibrar las dos dimensiones de Cuidar Personalmente y Desafiar Directamente.

text: During the 1-on-1 meeting, the manager noted: "While your code is robust, there is {{c1::room for improvement}} in your documentation. {{c2::Going forward}}, let's focus on adding README guides."
explanation: Room for improvement is a polite, constructive way to say that something needs development. **Going forward** is a transition phrase meaning 'in the future'.
usage:
Pattern: room for improvement in [area] / going forward, we should [action]
  • There is room for improvement in our test coverage.
  • Going forward, all code must be reviewed by two peers.
spanish: Durante la reunión 1 a 1, el gerente señaló: "Aunque tu código es robusto, hay margen de mejora en tu documentación. En el futuro, concentrémonos en agregar guías README".

text: I appreciate your hard work on this project; however, there is still {{c1::room for improvement}} in your documentation.
explanation: 'Room for improvement' is a polite, professional idiom meaning that something is not perfect and has potential to be better.
usage:
Pattern: room for improvement / areas of development
  • The report was good, but there's room for improvement.
  • There is always room for improvement in software performance.
spanish: Aprecio su arduo trabajo en este proyecto; sin embargo, todavía hay margen de mejora en su documentación.

text: To help you grow, I want to offer some {{c1::constructive feedback}} on your client presentation.
explanation: 'Constructive feedback' is supportive advice or critique designed to help someone improve their work or behavior, rather than simply pointing out faults.
usage:
Pattern: constructive feedback / constructive criticism
  • I always appreciate constructive feedback from my manager.
  • Try to phrase your comments as constructive feedback.
spanish: Para ayudarle a crecer, quiero ofrecerle retroalimentación constructiva sobre su presentación con el cliente.

text: You did a fantastic job managing the client's expectations; {{c1::keep up the good work}}!
explanation: 'Keep up the good work' is a common encouraging phrase used to praise someone's performance and motivate them to continue performing at a high level.
usage:
Pattern: keep up the good work / keep it up
  • The manager told us to keep up the good work.
  • Fantastic results this quarter, keep up the good work!
spanish: Hizo un trabajo fantástico gestionando las expectativas del cliente; ¡siga así (continúe con el buen trabajo)!

text: I've noticed that you've been missing deadlines; is there anything {{c1::holding you back}}?
explanation: To 'hold someone back' is a phrasal verb meaning to prevent someone from making progress, developing, or performing to their potential.
usage:
Pattern: hold [someone] back / block progress
  • What is holding you back from applying for the promotion?
  • Lack of funding is holding the research back.
spanish: He notado que ha estado perdiendo fechas límite; ¿hay algo que le impida avanzar/le esté frenando?

text: Let's set up a weekly check-in to track your progress and address any {{c1::pain points}} early.
explanation: A 'pain point' is a specific problem, obstacle, or difficulty experienced by employees, customers, or users.
usage:
Pattern: address pain points / resolve customer pain points
  • The new software addresses the main pain points of our team.
  • We need to identify user pain points in the checkout flow.
spanish: Establezcamos una reunión de control semanal para realizar un seguimiento de su progreso y abordar cualquier punto crítico/dificultad temprano.

text: Your technical skills are excellent, but you need to work on your {{c1::active listening}} during team meetings.
explanation: 'Active listening' is a communication technique that requires the listener to fully concentrate, understand, respond, and remember what is being said.
usage:
Pattern: practice active listening / active listening skills
  • Active listening is essential for effective conflict resolution.
  • She showed active listening by summarizing my points.
spanish: Sus habilidades técnicas son excelentes, pero necesita trabajar en su escucha activa durante las reuniones de equipo.

text: I want to recognize your effort; your dedication has made a {{c1::huge impact on}} the team's morale.
explanation: To 'make a huge impact on' means to have a very large, significant, or powerful effect or influence on a situation, group, or project.
usage:
Pattern: make a huge impact on [something]
  • The training made a huge impact on sales performance.
  • Your feedback has had a huge impact on my career growth.
spanish: Quiero reconocer su esfuerzo; su dedicación ha tenido un gran impacto en la moral del equipo.

text: I've noticed that you've been working late; please make sure you are balancing your workload to {{c1::avoid burnout}}.
explanation: To 'avoid burnout' means managing tasks, hours, and stress levels to prevent physical or mental collapse caused by overwork.
usage:
Pattern: avoid burnout / combat workplace stress
  • Taking regular breaks is crucial to avoid burnout.
  • Managers should monitor team workload to avoid burnout.
spanish: He notado que ha estado trabajando hasta tarde; por favor, asegúrese de equilibrar su carga de trabajo para evitar el agotamiento.

text: Your technical explanation was very clear, but you need to adapt your language when presenting to non-technical {{c1::audiences}}.
explanation: A 'non-technical audience' refers to clients, managers, or users who do not possess deep engineering or scientific knowledge, requiring simple language.
usage:
Pattern: non-technical audience / business stakeholders
  • Explain the algorithm simply for a non-technical audience.
  • The director is a non-technical audience, so skip the code snippets.
spanish: Su explicación técnica fue muy clara, pero debe adaptar su lenguaje al presentarse ante audiencias no técnicas.

text: I appreciate your initiative in organizing the files; it has saved the team a lot of {{c1::time}}.
explanation: To 'save time' means executing tasks efficiently to reduce the duration or effort required to complete a process.
usage:
Pattern: save time / time savings
  • The automation script saved the team hours of manual work.
  • We optimized the query to save execution time.
spanish: Agradezco su iniciativa al organizar los archivos; le ha ahorrado mucho tiempo al equipo.

text: To help you grow, let's identify some training courses that can help you build your {{c1::leadership skills}}.
explanation: 'Leadership skills' are the strengths and abilities individuals demonstrate when guiding processes, directing initiatives, and mentoring teammates.
usage:
Pattern: build leadership skills / leadership qualities
  • Effective communication is a core leadership skill.
  • The seminar focused on developing leadership skills in project managers.
spanish: Para ayudarle a crecer, identifiquemos algunos cursos de capacitación que puedan ayudarle a desarrollar sus habilidades de liderazgo.

text: Your coding standards are excellent, but you should try to be more proactive in code {{c1::reviews}}.
explanation: A 'code review' is the software development process where engineers inspect one another's source code to catch bugs and verify quality.
usage:
Pattern: code reviews / peer review
  • Be constructive when offering comments during code reviews.
  • We require two approvals on code reviews before merging.
spanish: Sus estándares de codificación son excelentes, pero debería intentar ser más proactivo en las revisiones de código.

text: I want to encourage you to share your ideas during brainstorming sessions; your perspective is {{c1::highly valued}}.
explanation: To be 'highly valued' means that a person's ideas, work, or presence is held in very high esteem and considered extremely important.
usage:
Pattern: highly valued / valued team member
  • Your contribution to the database migration was highly valued.
  • She is a highly valued member of our division.
spanish: Quiero animarle a compartir sus ideas durante las sesiones de lluvia de ideas; su perspectiva es muy valorada.

text: Let's work on improving your communication style to ensure updates are delivered in a timely {{c1::manner}}.
explanation: To deliver updates in a 'timely manner' (or timely fashion) means doing so quickly enough to be useful, before deadlines or issues arise.
usage:
Pattern: in a timely manner / timely fashion
  • Please submit the report in a timely manner.
  • The team resolved the incident in a timely manner.
spanish: Trabajemos en mejorar su estilo de comunicación para asegurar que las actualizaciones se entreguen de manera oportuna.

text: You did a great job handling that difficult client request; your patience was {{c1::exemplary}}.
explanation: 'Exemplary' is an adjective meaning serving as a desirable model; representing the best of its kind, worthy of imitation.
usage:
Pattern: exemplary behavior / exemplary work
  • Her dedication to quality is exemplary.
  • The audit praised the team's exemplary record-keeping.
spanish: Hizo un gran trabajo manejando esa difícil solicitud del cliente; su paciencia fue ejemplar.

text: I'd like to see you take on more responsibility in coordinating tasks for our next product {{c1::release}}.
explanation: A 'product release' is the distribution of a new or upgraded software application or feature set to users.
usage:
Pattern: product release / software release / version release
  • We are patching bugs before the final product release.
  • The marketing campaign is aligned with the product release date.
spanish: Me gustaría verle asumir más responsabilidad en la coordinación de tareas para nuestro próximo lanzamiento de producto.

text: I want to recognize your contribution to the team's success; your dedication has not gone {{c1::unnoticed}}.
explanation: To 'not go unnoticed' is a common professional idiom used to reassure an employee that managers have observed and appreciated their hard work.
usage:
Pattern: not go unnoticed / recognized effort
  • Your extra hours during the database outage did not go unnoticed.
  • Her efforts to improve team morale have not gone unnoticed.
spanish: Quiero reconocer su contribución al éxito del equipo; su dedicación no ha pasado inadvertida.

text: When a client objects to a price increase, you can say: "I hear your {{c1::concerns regarding the cost}}; however, this new tier {{c2::delivers significant value by}} doubling your API rate limit."
explanation: Acknowledging the objection first shows respect, while 'delivers value by' steers the conversation toward benefits rather than expense.
usage:
Pattern: I hear your concerns regarding [topic]; however, this [solution] delivers value by [benefit]
  • I hear your concerns regarding the timeline; however, this phase delivers value by securing your data early.
  • We believe this update delivers significant value by reducing server load.
spanish: Cuando un cliente se opone a un aumento de precio, puedes decir: "Escucho sus inquietudes con respecto al costo; sin embargo, este nuevo nivel ofrece un valor significativo al duplicar su límite de velocidad de API".

text: If a user rejects a solution, offer options: "If that option doesn't {{c1::align with your objectives}}, we can {{c2::explore a phased roll-out}} instead."
explanation: Align with objectives means to match their goals. **Explore a phased roll-out** means to implement the solution in gradual steps rather than all at once.
usage:
Pattern: If [solution] doesn't align with your objectives, we can explore [alternative]
  • If the standard plan doesn't align with your objectives, we can explore a custom contract.
  • Let's explore a phased roll-out of the security policy.
spanish: Si un usuario rechaza una solución, ofrece opciones: "Si esa opción no se alinea con sus objetivos, podemos explorar una implementación gradual en su lugar".

text: To uncover the root of a client's hesitation: "What is your {{c1::primary reservation about}} moving forward with this integration?"
explanation: Primary reservation refers to the main doubt, worry, or objection holding someone back from making a decision.
usage:
Pattern: What is your primary reservation about [action]?
  • What is your primary reservation about migrating to the cloud?
  • His primary reservation about the project was the tight deadline.
spanish: Para descubrir la raíz de la vacilación de un cliente: "¿Cuál es su principal reserva sobre seguir adelante con esta integración?"

text: When a customer compares you to a competitor: "I understand that [Competitor] has {{c1::a lower price point}}, but our platform provides {{c2::enterprise-grade security}} that is not offered elsewhere."
explanation: Lower price point is a professional euphemism for 'cheaper'. **Enterprise-grade** denotes high-quality, robust features suited for large, demanding organizations.
usage:
Pattern: understand [competitor] has a lower price point, but our [product] provides [advantage]
  • I understand they have a lower price point, but our service provides 99.99% uptime.
  • We need enterprise-grade security to satisfy compliance requirements.
spanish: Cuando un cliente te compara con un competidor: "Entiendo que [Competidor] tiene un precio más bajo, pero nuestra plataforma ofrece seguridad de nivel empresarial que no se ofrece en otros lugares".

text: I understand your concern about the budget, but let me show you how this tool will {{c1::save you money}} in the long run.
explanation: To 'save money' means to reduce expenses or prevent financial waste, a key value proposition in professional sales.
usage:
Pattern: save money / cost savings
  • Moving to the cloud will save us money on infrastructure.
  • This energy-efficient device will save you money.
spanish: Entiendo su preocupación sobre el presupuesto, pero permítame mostrarle cómo esta herramienta le ahorrará dinero a largo plazo.

text: That's a valid point; however, our software integrates seamlessly with your {{c1::legacy systems}}.
explanation: A 'legacy system' is an old or outdated computer system, technology, or software application that a company continues to use because it is critical to their business.
usage:
Pattern: legacy systems / legacy technology
  • Replacing legacy systems can be extremely expensive and risky.
  • We built an API wrapper to connect our app to their legacy system.
spanish: Ese es un punto válido; sin embargo, nuestro software se integra a la perfección con sus sistemas heredados/antiguos.

text: To address your security concerns, we are fully compliant with all major {{c1::industry standards}}.
explanation: An 'industry standard' is a technical specification, requirement, or guideline widely accepted and followed in a specific field (e.g. ISO 27001, SOC 2).
usage:
Pattern: industry standards / compliance standards
  • Our security protocols meet the highest industry standards.
  • We must update our process to keep up with changing industry standards.
spanish: Para abordar sus preocupaciones de seguridad, cumplimos plenamente con todos los principales estándares de la industria.

text: I hear what you are saying, but our customer support team is available {{c1::around the clock}} to help you.
explanation: 'Around the clock' is an idiom meaning continuously, 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.
usage:
Pattern: around the clock / 24/7 support
  • The engineering team worked around the clock to fix the database.
  • We offer customer service around the clock.
spanish: Escucho lo que dice, pero nuestro equipo de soporte al cliente está disponible las 24 horas del día para ayudarle.

text: We can adjust the project scope to fit your timeline if that is the {{c1::deciding factor}} for you.
explanation: The 'deciding factor' is the key element, reason, or condition that makes a person resolve a choice or make a final decision.
usage:
Pattern: deciding factor / deal-breaker
  • Price was the deciding factor in our choice of vendor.
  • Safety features were the deciding factor when buying the car.
spanish: Podemos ajustar el alcance del proyecto para adaptarlo a su cronograma si ese es el factor decisivo para usted.

text: Let me walk you through our case studies to show how we solved similar problems for {{c1::other clients}}.
explanation: A 'case study' is a detailed analysis of a project or campaign, used as marketing evidence to show potential clients how a company successfully delivered value.
usage:
Pattern: case study / client success story
  • We published three new case studies on our website.
  • Our case studies prove that our algorithm improves conversion rates.
spanish: Permítame guiarle a través de nuestros estudios de caso para mostrar cómo resolvimos problemas similares para otros clientes.

text: I understand that our service is more expensive, but it includes features that our competitors charge {{c1::extra for}}.
explanation: To charge 'extra for [something]' means requiring additional payment for specific premium add-ons or services.
usage:
Pattern: charge extra for [add-on]
  • Many airlines charge extra for checked bags.
  • We do not charge extra for technical support.
spanish: Entiendo que nuestro servicio es más costoso, pero incluye características por las que nuestros competidores cobran un cargo extra.

text: To address your concerns about downtime, we offer a service level agreement (SLA) with a 99.9% {{c1::uptime guarantee}}.
explanation: An 'uptime guarantee' is a contractual promise by a hosting or software vendor specifying the minimum percentage of time a service will be operational.
usage:
Pattern: uptime guarantee / guaranteed uptime
  • Our cloud provider offers a 99.99% uptime guarantee.
  • Violating the uptime guarantee results in service credits for the client.
spanish: Para abordar sus preocupaciones sobre el tiempo de inactividad, ofrecemos un acuerdo de nivel de servicio (SLA) con una garantía de tiempo de actividad del 99.9%.

text: Our onboarding process is designed to minimize friction; we will guide your team through every {{c1::step}}.
explanation: A 'step' is a single action or phase in a sequence of events designed to achieve a goal (like onboarding).
usage:
Pattern: step by step / sequential steps
  • Follow the installation steps carefully.
  • What is the next step in our database migration?
spanish: Nuestro proceso de incorporación está diseñado para minimizar la fricción; guiaremos a su equipo a través de cada paso.

text: I appreciate your concern about the implementation timeline, but our dedicated support team will handle the {{c1::heavy lifting}}.
explanation: 'Heavy lifting' is an idiom meaning the most difficult, labor-intensive, or complex part of a task or project.
usage:
Pattern: do the heavy lifting / handle heavy lifting
  • Our scripts do the heavy lifting of parsing logs automatically.
  • The consultant handled the heavy lifting during corporate restructuring.
spanish: Agradezco su preocupación sobre el cronograma de implementación, pero nuestro equipo de soporte dedicado se encargará del trabajo pesado.

text: We can start with a small pilot project to demonstrate the value of our tool before you commit to a {{c1::full contract}}.
explanation: A 'pilot project' (or pilot study) is a small-scale, short-term experiment conducted to evaluate feasibility, cost, and utility before full deployment.
usage:
Pattern: pilot project / pilot study / trial run
  • We launched a pilot project with three departments first.
  • The pilot project was highly successful, leading to full contract approval.
spanish: Podemos comenzar con un pequeño proyecto piloto para demostrar el valor de nuestra herramienta antes de que se comprometa con un contrato completo.

text: If safety is your main concern, our data servers are protected by military-grade encryption and regular {{c1::audits}}.
explanation: An 'audit' is an official inspection of an organization's accounts, security status, or processes, typically conducted by an independent body.
usage:
Pattern: security audits / regular compliance audits
  • Our security protocols are verified by annual third-party audits.
  • We must prepare the documents for the financial audit next month.
spanish: Si la seguridad es su principal preocupación, nuestros servidores de datos están protegidos por cifrado de grado militar y auditorías regulares.

text: We have client testimonials from companies in your industry showing how they improved efficiency by {{c1::40%}}.
explanation: A 'testimonial' is a formal statement testifying to someone's character, qualifications, or the value of a product/service, written by a satisfied customer.
usage:
Pattern: client testimonials / customer reviews
  • The sales deck includes several testimonials from enterprise clients.
  • Client testimonials help build trust with prospective buyers.
spanish: Tenemos testimonios de clientes de empresas en su industria que muestran cómo mejoraron la eficiencia en un 40%.

text: I hear what you are saying about resource constraints; that is why our tool requires almost zero {{c1::maintenance}}.
explanation: 'Maintenance' refers to the process of preserving a system, software, or building in good working condition through regular checks and repairs.
usage:
Pattern: zero maintenance / low maintenance / routine maintenance
  • Our SaaS platform handles updates automatically, ensuring zero maintenance for you.
  • Database index maintenance is scheduled weekly.
spanish: Escucho lo que dice sobre las limitaciones de recursos; es por eso que nuestra herramienta requiere casi cero mantenimiento.

text: We offer flexible contract terms that allow you to scale down your usage if your business needs {{c1::change}}.
explanation: To 'change' is the verb meaning to make or become different, commonly used to describe shifting business requirements.
usage:
Pattern: needs change / plans change
  • Our database schema must adapt as user needs change.
  • Business plans changed due to the economic downturn.
spanish: Ofrecemos condiciones de contrato flexibles que le permiten reducir su uso si las necesidades de su negocio cambian.

text: Let's schedule a demo session next week so your team can test the interface and see how {{c1::user-friendly}} it is.
explanation: 'User-friendly' describes an interface, software, or device that is easy to learn, understand, and navigate for consumers.
usage:
Pattern: user-friendly interface / user-friendly design
  • The new website layout is much more user-friendly.
  • We prioritized user-friendly navigation in our mobile app.
spanish: Programemos una sesión de demostración la próxima semana para que su equipo pueda probar la interfaz y ver qué tan fácil de usar es.

text: During the interview, she explained: "I {{c1::spearheaded the optimization}} of our CI/CD pipelines, which {{c2::streamlined our deployment cycle}} by 40%."
explanation: Spearhead is an active, high-impact verb meaning to lead an initiative. **Streamline** means to make a process more efficient by removing bottlenecks.
usage:
Pattern: spearhead the [action] of [system] / streamline [process]
  • He spearheaded the migration of our legacy systems.
  • We need to streamline our support ticketing process.
