spanish: Durante la entrevista, ella explicó: "Lideré la optimización de nuestros pipelines de CI/CD, lo que simplificó nuestro ciclo de implementación en un 40%".

text: Could you walk me through your background and describe a project you are {{c1::particularly proud of}}?
explanation: To be 'particularly proud of' means to feel a deep sense of satisfaction or accomplishment regarding a specific work piece or result.
usage:
Pattern: particularly proud of [project / achievement]
  • I am particularly proud of the database migration we completed last month.
  • She is particularly proud of her team's growth.
spanish: ¿Podría guiarme a través de su trayectoria y describir un proyecto del cual esté especialmente orgulloso?

text: When faced with tight deadlines, how do you prioritize tasks and manage {{c1::under pressure}}?
explanation: To manage or work 'under pressure' means to maintain high performance, focus, and emotional control during high-stress conditions.
usage:
Pattern: work under pressure / perform under pressure
  • Our ideal candidate must be able to work under pressure.
  • I have learned to stay calm and organized under pressure.
spanish: Cuando se enfrenta a plazos de entrega ajustados, ¿cómo prioriza las tareas y se desenvuelve bajo presión?

text: I am looking for a role where I can apply my skills and grow {{c1::within the company}}.
explanation: To grow 'within the company' means to advance your career path, receive promotions, and take on new roles inside the same organization over time.
usage:
Pattern: grow within the company / internal promotion
  • There are many opportunities to grow within the company.
  • I want to build a long-term career and grow within the company.
spanish: Estoy buscando un puesto donde pueda aplicar mis habilidades y crecer dentro de la empresa.

text: Why are you interested in joining our team, and what do you know about our {{c1::core values}}?
explanation: 'Core values' are the fundamental beliefs and guiding principles that dictate behavior, decisions, and culture inside an organization.
usage:
Pattern: core values / company culture
  • Our core values include integrity, innovation, and teamwork.
  • Aligning with the company's core values is essential.
spanish: ¿Por qué está interesado en unirse a nuestro equipo, y qué sabe sobre nuestros valores fundamentales?

text: Could you give me an example of a time you had a conflict with a coworker and how you {{c1::resolved it}}?
explanation: To 'resolve a conflict' means to settle a disagreement, dispute, or friction between coworkers in a professional, constructive manner.
usage:
Pattern: resolve a conflict / settle a dispute
  • We resolved the conflict by scheduling a private discussion.
  • HR helps employees resolve conflicts in the office.
spanish: ¿Podría darme un ejemplo de alguna ocasión en la que haya tenido un conflicto con un compañero de trabajo y cómo lo resolvió?

text: I am comfortable working in both collaborative team environments and {{c1::independently}}.
explanation: To work 'independently' means to execute tasks, make decisions, and manage your time without constant supervision or direction.
usage:
Pattern: work independently / self-directed
  • This role requires someone who can work independently with minimal guidance.
  • He is capable of working independently on complex tasks.
spanish: Me siento cómodo trabajando tanto en entornos de equipo colaborativos como de manera independiente.

text: What are your salary expectations for this position, and do you have any {{c1::flexibility}}?
explanation: 'Salary expectations' refer to the target compensation range an applicant wants to earn in a new job. Asking about 'flexibility' queries if they are open to negotiation.
usage:
Pattern: salary expectations / target compensation
  • My salary expectations are in the range of $80,000 to $90,000.
  • We discussed salary expectations during the initial phone screening.
spanish: ¿Cuáles son sus expectativas salariales para este puesto, y tiene alguna flexibilidad?

text: Thank you for your time today; I look forward to hearing about the {{c1::next steps}} in the process.
explanation: The 'next steps' in a recruiting process refer to the subsequent phases (e.g. technical interview, reference checks, final offer).
usage:
Pattern: next steps / follow-up process
  • What are the next steps in your hiring process?
  • The recruiter will contact you regarding the next steps.
spanish: Gracias por su tiempo hoy; espero con interés saber de los siguientes pasos en el proceso.

text: Do you have any questions for us about the role or the team {{c1::dynamics}}?
explanation: 'Team dynamics' refer to the psychological forces and behavioral relationships that influence how a team communicates and collaborates.
usage:
Pattern: team dynamics / working relationships
  • I want to ask about the team dynamics and how tasks are assigned.
  • Resolving conflict is crucial to maintaining positive team dynamics.
spanish: ¿Tiene alguna pregunta para nosotros sobre el puesto o la dinámica del equipo?

text: I am looking for a position that offers challenges and allows me to expand my technical {{c1::expertise}}.
explanation: 'Expertise' refers to highly specialized skill, knowledge, or experience in a particular domain (e.g. database administration).
usage:
Pattern: technical expertise / domain expertise
  • We need consultants with cloud security expertise.
  • She demonstrated her technical expertise during the live coding test.
spanish: Estoy buscando un puesto que ofrezca desafíos y me permita ampliar mi experiencia/conocimiento técnico.

text: Could you describe a time when you made a mistake at work and how you handled the {{c1::consequences}}?
explanation: 'Consequences' are the results, outcomes, or effects of an action or event, typically bad or unpleasant outcomes from a mistake.
usage:
Pattern: handle the consequences / face the consequences
  • The database crash had severe financial consequences.
  • He was willing to accept the consequences of his actions.
spanish: ¿Podría describir una ocasión en la que cometió un error en el trabajo y cómo manejó las consecuencias?

text: I have experience working in fast-paced environments and can adapt to changing priorities {{c1::quickly}}.
explanation: 'Quickly' is an adverb meaning at a fast speed or in a short duration of time, essential for agile workspaces.
usage:
Pattern: adapt quickly / learn quickly
  • New hires must adapt quickly to our workflow.
  • The system scaled up quickly to handle the traffic.
spanish: Tengo experiencia trabajando en entornos de ritmo rápido y puedo adaptarme a las prioridades cambiantes rápidamente.

text: Why do you think you are the best fit for this role, and what unique skills can you bring to our {{c1::team}}?
explanation: A 'team' is a collaborative group of people working together to achieve a common goal or project.
usage:
Pattern: join our team / team player
  • I am excited to join your development team.
  • He is a great team player who supports his colleagues.
spanish: ¿Por qué cree que es la persona más adecuada para este puesto, y qué habilidades únicas puede aportar a nuestro equipo?

text: Could you give me an example of a successful project you managed and how you measured its {{c1::success}}?
explanation: 'Success' refers to the accomplishment of an aim, purpose, or target (such as project completion on time and within budget).
usage:
Pattern: measure success / project success
  • We measured the project's success using key performance indicators.
  • The launch was a huge success.
spanish: ¿Podría darme un ejemplo de algún proyecto exitoso que haya gestionado y cómo midió su éxito?

text: I am comfortable presenting data to senior managers and explaining complex concepts in simple {{c1::terms}}.
explanation: To explain something in 'simple terms' means translating highly technical jargon into clear, straightforward language that non-experts can understand.
usage:
Pattern: in simple terms / layman's terms
  • Explain how backpropagation works in simple terms.
  • In simple terms, a VPC is an isolated network.
spanish: Me siento cómodo presentando datos a directores sénior y explicando conceptos complejos en términos sencillos.

text: What are your professional goals for the next three years, and how does this role align with {{c1::them}}?
explanation: 'Them' is the third-person plural object pronoun, here referencing the applicant's professional goals.
usage:
Pattern: align with them / work towards them
  • These objectives are important, and my tasks align with them.
  • I set goals and did my best to achieve them.
spanish: ¿Cuáles son sus metas profesionales para los próximos tres años, y cómo se alinea este puesto con ellas?

text: I appreciate this opportunity to speak with you today; I am very excited about this role and the {{c1::company's vision}}.
explanation: The 'company's vision' (or vision statement) is a corporate declaration of the firm's long-term goals and values, inspiring staff and clients.
usage:
Pattern: company's vision / mission statement
  • Our company's vision is to make AI accessible to everyone.
  • I want to work for a firm with a clear company vision.
spanish: Agradezco esta oportunidad de hablar con usted hoy; estoy muy entusiasmado con este puesto y la visión de la empresa.

text: Could you tell me more about the company culture and how the team collaborates on a daily {{c1::basis}}?
explanation: To do something 'on a daily basis' (or daily) is a standard business idiom meaning regularly every day.
usage:
Pattern: on a daily basis / regular basis
  • We run database sync scripts on a daily basis.
  • I check my inbox on a daily basis.
spanish: ¿Podría contarme más sobre la cultura de la empresa y cómo colabora el equipo a diario?

text: Thank you for the detailed explanation; I have no further questions at this time and look forward to your {{c1::feedback}}.
explanation: 'Feedback' in a recruitment context refers to the interview evaluation or decision sent by recruiters or managers to an applicant.
usage:
Pattern: receive feedback / interview feedback
  • The recruiter promised to send interview feedback by Friday.
  • Constructive feedback helps candidates improve.
spanish: Gracias por la explicación detallada; no tengo más preguntas en este momento y espero con interés sus comentarios/respuesta.

text: Could you please {{c1::power cycle}} the router by unplugging it for 30 seconds?
explanation: power cycle is a technical verb meaning to turn an electronic device completely off (usually by cutting power) and back on again to reset it.
usage:
Pattern: power cycle [device] (e.g. power cycle the server, power cycle the modem)
  • If your connection drops, try power cycling the access point.
  • I power cycled the switch, which solved the IP conflict.
spanish: ¿Podrías por favor apagar y encender el router desenchufándolo durante 30 segundos?

text: We are currently experiencing a {{c1::service disruption}} affecting our primary database, and our team is working to mitigate it.
explanation: service disruption is the formal corporate term for an outage or downtime. mitigate means to reduce the severity or impact of an ongoing incident.
usage:
Pattern: experience a service disruption | mitigate [incident/impact/risk]
  • The network migration caused a minor service disruption for 10 minutes.
  • We are deploying a hotfix to mitigate the database CPU spikes.
spanish: Actualmente estamos experimentando una interrupción del servicio que afecta a nuestra base de datos principal, y nuestro equipo está trabajando para mitigarlo.

text: The engineer reported: "We are experiencing a major {{c1::infrastructure outage}} due to a {{c2::database bottleneck}}, and we are preparing a root cause analysis."
explanation: Outage means service downtime. **Bottleneck** refers to a point of congestion in a system that slows down performance. **Root cause analysis (RCA)** is the diagnostic process to find the source of the issue.
usage:
Pattern: experience an outage due to [cause] / conduct a root cause analysis
  • The network outage was caused by a configuration error.
  • We conducted a root cause analysis to prevent future failures.
spanish: El ingeniero informó: "Estamos experimentando una interrupción importante de la infraestructura debido a un cuello de botella en la base de datos, y estamos preparando un análisis de causa raíz".

text: Have you tried turning the device off and on again to {{c1::power cycle}} it?
explanation: To 'power cycle' a device means to shut off the power to a hardware piece and then turn it back on, which clears memory and resets state.
usage:
Pattern: power cycle / reboot the router
  • IT support always asks you to power cycle the modem first.
  • Power cycling the switch resolved the packet loss.
spanish: ¿Ha intentado apagar y volver a encender el dispositivo para reiniciar el ciclo de energía?

text: I will open a support ticket for this issue, and our team will {{c1::look into it}} shortly.
explanation: To 'look into' is a common phrasal verb meaning to investigate, examine, or check the status of a problem or request.
usage:
Pattern: look into [issue / ticket / bug]
  • We are looking into the server latency report.
  • I'll look into why your payment was declined.
spanish: Abriré un ticket de soporte para este problema, y nuestro equipo lo revisará/investigará en breve.

text: Please make sure your security credentials are up to date to prevent login {{c1::lockouts}}.
explanation: A 'lockout' is a security state where a user is temporarily or permanently barred from accessing an account after too many failed password attempts.
usage:
Pattern: account lockout / lockout policy
  • Three failed attempts will trigger an automatic account lockout.
  • Contact IT support to resolve your password lockout.
spanish: Por favor, asegúrese de que sus credenciales de seguridad estén actualizadas para evitar bloqueos de inicio de sesión.

text: We are currently experiencing a temporary server outage; we apologize for the {{c1::downtime}}.
explanation: 'Downtime' refers to the period during which a system, computer network, or server is unavailable or offline.
usage:
Pattern: server downtime / system downtime
  • Scheduled maintenance downtime will occur this Sunday.
  • Our service level agreement guarantees less than 0.01% downtime.
spanish: Actualmente estamos experimentando una interrupción temporal del servidor; nos disculpamos por el tiempo de inactividad.

text: Could you please send me a screenshot of the error message to help us {{c1::troubleshoot}} the problem?
explanation: To 'troubleshoot' means to identify, analyze, and resolve technical faults or bugs in a system by following a logical process of elimination.
usage:
Pattern: troubleshoot the problem / technical troubleshooting
  • We need to troubleshoot why the API is returning 500 errors.
  • He is very good at troubleshooting network connection problems.
spanish: ¿Podría por favor enviarme una captura de pantalla del mensaje de error para ayudarnos a diagnosticar/resolver el problema?

text: You need to update your operating system to the latest version to patch these {{c1::security vulnerabilities}}.
explanation: A 'security vulnerability' is a flaw or weakness in software or hardware that malicious actors can exploit to gain unauthorized access or execute code.
usage:
Pattern: patch security vulnerabilities / zero-day vulnerability
  • The patch fixes critical security vulnerabilities in the kernel.
  • Cybercriminals scan the web looking for unpatched security vulnerabilities.
spanish: Necesita actualizar su sistema operativo a la última versión para parchear estas vulnerabilidades de seguridad.

text: Please check your spam folder if you haven't received the password reset link {{c1::in your inbox}}.
explanation: An 'inbox' is the folder where newly received email messages are stored.
usage:
Pattern: in your inbox / email inbox
  • The confirmation code should arrive in your inbox in a minute.
  • My inbox is flooded with spam emails.
spanish: Por favor, revise su carpeta de correo no deseado (spam) si no ha recibido el enlace de restablecimiento de contraseña en su bandeja de entrada.

text: Could you please clear your browser cache and cookies to see if that resolves the display {{c1::glitch}}?
explanation: A 'glitch' is a minor, temporary, or sudden malfunction or technical fault in software, hardware, or a system.
usage:
Pattern: display glitch / software glitch
  • A temporary network glitch interrupted the database backup.
  • The developer patched the layout glitch in the landing page.
spanish: ¿Podría por favor borrar la caché y las cookies de su navegador para ver si eso resuelve el fallo técnico de visualización?

text: I will need to remote into your desktop to inspect the network configuration; do I have your {{c1::permission}}?
explanation: 'Permission' is the formal consent or authorization to do something, required in IT support before accessing client devices.
usage:
Pattern: request permission / access permission
  • The agent asked for permission to install the updates.
  • You do not have permission to access this directory.
spanish: Necesitaré acceder de forma remota a su escritorio para inspeccionar la configuración de la red; ¿tengo su permiso?

text: Please make sure your VPN connection is active before trying to access the internal database {{c1::servers}}.
explanation: A 'server' is a computer program or device that provides functionality or database storage for other programs or devices called clients.
usage:
Pattern: database servers / cloud servers
  • The database servers are hosted in a secure VPC.
  • We migrated our production servers to Google Cloud.
spanish: Por favor, asegúrese de que su conexión VPN esté activa antes de intentar acceder a los servidores de la base de datos interna.

text: We are upgrading our server infrastructure this weekend, so expect some temporary service {{c1::disruptions}}.
explanation: A 'disruption' is an event or activity that interrupts a process, system, or service from operating normally.
usage:
Pattern: service disruptions / temporary disruption
  • The storm caused major power disruptions in the city.
  • Minimize service disruptions by performing updates during off-peak hours.
spanish: Estamos actualizando nuestra infraestructura de servidores este fin de semana, por lo que puede esperar algunas interrupciones temporales del servicio.

text: Your ticket has been assigned to our network security specialist, and they will contact you to {{c1::resolve the issue}}.
explanation: To 'resolve the issue' is the standard formal collocation meaning to fix a technical problem, bug, or ticket successfully.
usage:
Pattern: resolve the issue / solve the problem
  • Our priority is to resolve the issue as quickly as possible.
  • IT support resolved the login issue in minutes.
spanish: Su ticket ha sido asignado a nuestro especialista en seguridad de red, y se pondrá en contacto con usted para resolver el problema.

text: Could you please verify that all cables are securely plugged into the docking station and the {{c1::monitor}}?
explanation: A 'monitor' is the computer screen that displays video and graphics output from a computer's processor.
usage:
Pattern: computer monitor / dual-monitor setup
  • I set up a second monitor to improve my coding workflow.
  • The monitor went black because the power cord was loose.
spanish: ¿Podría por favor verificar que todos los cables estén bien conectados a la estación de acoplamiento y al monitor?

text: We have reset your password; please use the temporary credentials to log in and create a new {{c1::password}}.
explanation: A 'password' is a secret word or string of characters used to verify identity and gain access to a computer system or account.
usage:
Pattern: create a password / strong password
  • A strong password should include letters, numbers, and symbols.
  • I forgot my password and had to request a reset.
spanish: Hemos restablecido su contraseña; por favor use las credenciales temporales para iniciar sesión y crear una nueva contraseña.

text: To prevent cyberattacks, all employees must complete the mandatory phishing awareness training by {{c1::Friday}}.
explanation: 'Friday' is the fifth day of the standard workweek, commonly set as a deadline for business training courses.
usage:
Pattern: by Friday / Friday afternoon
  • Please submit the timesheet by Friday.
  • The office closes early on Friday.
spanish: Para prevenir ataques cibernéticos, todos los empleados deben completar la capacitación obligatoria sobre concientización de phishing para el viernes.

text: Your computer has been flagged for using outdated software; please run the system updates {{c1::immediately}}.
explanation: 'Immediately' is an adverb meaning right away, without any delay or pause, highlighting urgency in security compliance.
usage:
Pattern: immediately / without delay
  • If you see a security warning, contact IT support immediately.
  • The server was shut down immediately after the breach.
spanish: Su computadora ha sido marcada por usar software desactualizado; por favor ejecute las actualizaciones del sistema de inmediato.

text: If the issue persists, please reply to this email thread or contact our IT support hotline directly at {{c1::extension 500}}.
explanation: A phone 'extension' is a secondary telephone line connected to a main switchboard in an office, identified by a short number.
usage:
Pattern: extension [number] / direct extension
  • Dial the main office and enter extension 234.
  • What is your direct extension?
spanish: Si el problema persiste, responda a este hilo de correo electrónico o póngase en contacto directamente con nuestra línea directa de soporte de TI en la extensión 500.

text: I’ll take {{c1::ownership}} of this and keep you updated.
explanation: Ownership is one of the most important leadership expressions in professional English. It conveys responsibility and follow-through.
usage:
Pattern: take ownership of
  • I’ll take ownership of the rollout.
  • She took ownership of the incident.
spanish: Me haré cargo de esto y te mantendré informado.

text: Let’s make sure we’re all {{c1::aligned}} on the priorities.
explanation: Aligned means working toward the same objective. It is highly common in management and leadership conversations.
usage:
Pattern: aligned on priorities / aligned on goals
  • We are aligned on the roadmap.
  • Make sure the team is aligned.
spanish: Asegurémonos de que todos estemos alineados en las prioridades.

text: I wanted to {{c1::escalate}} this because it affects the timeline.
explanation: Escalate is a formal leadership phrase used when a problem needs higher attention or authority.
usage:
Pattern: escalate a concern / escalate an issue
  • I need to escalate this issue.
  • Please escalate it to the manager.
spanish: Quería elevar esto porque afecta el calendario.

text: Based on the data, I think we should {{c1::move forward}} with option two.
explanation: Move forward is a common leadership phrase meaning to proceed with a plan.
usage:
Pattern: move forward with
  • Let’s move forward with the launch.
  • We can move forward once the review is done.
spanish: Basado en los datos, creo que deberíamos avanzar con la opción dos.

text: The main {{c1::risk}} is that we might miss the deadline if we delay again.
explanation: Risk is a central word in leadership and operations communication. It is often used to discuss uncertainty and consequences.
usage:
Pattern: main risk / key risk
  • The main risk is quality.
  • This creates a significant risk.
spanish: El principal riesgo es que podríamos perder la fecha límite si retrasamos otra vez.

text: Trust comes from being {{c1::transparent}} and consistent over time.
explanation: Transparent means honest and open. It is one of the most valued leadership qualities.
usage:
Pattern: be transparent / transparent communication
  • We need transparent communication.
  • Transparency builds trust.
spanish: La confianza surge de ser transparente y coherente con el tiempo.

text: I'd like you to {{c1::take the lead on}} this integration, but feel free to escalate if you hit any roadblocks.
explanation: take the lead on means to assume responsibility for directing or managing a specific project. escalate means to report a problem up the chain of command when tools or resources are blocked.
usage:
Patterns: take the lead on [project/task] | escalate to [someone]
  • She will take the lead on the customer migration.
  • If the vendor does not respond, escalate the ticket to me.
spanish: Me gustaría que lideres esta integración, pero no dudes en escalar si te encuentras con algún obstáculo.

text: It is my responsibility to delegate tasks and ensure my team has the support they need to {{c1::meet their targets}}.
explanation: To 'meet targets' means to achieve the specific goals, performance levels, or metrics set by company leadership.
usage:
Pattern: meet targets / achieve goals
  • The sales team worked hard to meet their targets this quarter.
  • We did not meet our performance targets last year.
spanish: Es mi responsabilidad delegar tareas y asegurar que mi equipo tenga el apoyo que necesita para cumplir con sus metas/objetivos.

text: We need to hold weekly one-on-one meetings to check in on progress and remove any {{c1::blockers}}.
explanation: A 'blocker' (or impediment) is an issue, bug, or dependency that prevents a team member from making progress on their tasks.
usage:
Pattern: remove blockers / clear roadblocks
  • My primary job as a manager is to remove blockers for my devs.
  • Do you have any blockers keeping you from finishing the script?
spanish: Necesitamos realizar reuniones individuales semanales para revisar el progreso y eliminar cualquier bloqueador/impedimento.

text: My goal is to support my team's professional development and help them {{c1::reach their full potential}}.
explanation: To 'reach your full potential' means to develop your talents, skills, and career capabilities to the maximum possible level.
usage:
Pattern: reach full potential / unlock potential
  • A good leader helps their employees reach their full potential.
  • Mentorship is key to helping junior devs reach their full potential.
spanish: Mi objetivo es apoyar el desarrollo profesional de mi equipo y ayudarles a alcanzar su máximo potencial.

text: We must manage our team's workload carefully to prevent burnout and maintain high {{c1::morale}}.
explanation: 'Morale' refers to the enthusiasm, confidence, and mental state of a group or team regarding their tasks and work environment.
usage:
Pattern: boost morale / low morale
  • Missing targets led to low morale in the sales department.
  • The team-building event was organized to boost morale.
spanish: Debemos gestionar el volumen de trabajo de nuestro equipo cuidadosamente para evitar el agotamiento y mantener la moral alta.

text: I need to coordinate with other department heads to align our resource allocation and {{c1::avoid duplication of effort}}.
explanation: To 'avoid duplication of effort' means to organize work so that two different people or teams do not spend resources doing the exact same task independently.
usage:
Pattern: avoid duplication of effort / streamline work
  • We merged the codebases to avoid duplication of effort.
  • Communication is essential to avoid duplication of effort in large projects.
spanish: Necesito coordinar con otros jefes de departamento para alinear nuestra asignación de recursos y evitar la duplicación de esfuerzos.

text: We will recognize outstanding performance by offering bonuses and public {{c1::praise}}.
explanation: 'Praise' refers to expressions of approval, commendation, or admiration. Offering 'public praise' honors someone's achievements in front of their peers.
usage:
Pattern: public praise / praise employees
  • Praise in public and criticize in private.
  • The CEO offered public praise to the migration team.
spanish: Reconoceremos el rendimiento sobresaliente ofreciendo bonos y elogios/reconocimiento público.

text: I need to review our team's performance data and present the findings to senior {{c1::stakeholders}}.
explanation: A 'stakeholder' is anyone who has an interest or investment in a business or project. 'Senior stakeholders' typically refer to directors, executives, or clients.
usage:
Pattern: senior stakeholders / key stakeholders
  • We must keep our senior stakeholders informed of project delays.
  • The requirements were defined by the key stakeholders.
spanish: Necesito revisar los datos de rendimiento de nuestro equipo y presentar los hallazgos a las partes interesadas/directivos sénior.

text: We must establish clear performance guidelines so everyone knows what is {{c1::expected of them}}.
explanation: What is 'expected of someone' refers to the tasks, behaviors, or results they are responsible for delivering in their role.
usage:
Pattern: expected of [someone] / job expectations
  • You did not meet what was expected of you this month.
  • Clear communication helps everyone understand what is expected of them.
spanish: Debemos establecer pautas de rendimiento claras para que todos sepan qué es esperado de ellos.

text: I am here to support you; please let me know if you need to {{c1::escalate}} any issues.
explanation: To 'escalate' an issue means to refer a problem or task to a higher authority or senior manager because it cannot be resolved at the current level.
usage:
Pattern: escalate issues / escalate a ticket
  • If the client is still unhappy, escalate the call to me.
  • We had to escalate the database outage to the operations director.
spanish: Estoy aquí para apoyarle; por favor hágame saber si necesita escalar/elevar a un nivel superior cualquier problema.

text: It is my goal to ensure my team members have a healthy work-life balance and feel {{c1::valued}}.
explanation: To feel 'valued' means feeling that your contributions, ideas, and presence are recognized, appreciated, and respected by managers.
usage:
Pattern: feel valued / appreciated employee
  • Praising employees in public helps them feel valued.
  • If workers don't feel valued, they will leave the company.
spanish: Mi meta es asegurar que los miembros de mi equipo tengan un equilibrio saludable entre el trabajo y la vida personal y se sientan valorados.

text: We need to set up performance reviews next month to discuss career goals and identify areas for {{c1::growth}}.
explanation: 'Career growth' (or professional growth) refers to climbing the corporate ladder, learning new skills, and taking on more responsibility.
usage:
Pattern: career growth / professional development
  • This role offers excellent opportunities for career growth.
  • Mentorship is essential for employee career growth.
spanish: Necesitamos programar evaluaciones de desempeño el próximo mes para discutir las metas profesionales e identificar áreas de crecimiento.

text: My responsibility is to manage the project budget and ensure we do not exceed our allocated {{c1::spending limits}}.
explanation: A 'spending limit' is a strict maximum amount of money allocated for a specific project, department, or transaction.
usage:
Pattern: spending limits / budget constraints
  • The director set strict spending limits on travel.
  • We must monitor expenses to stay within our spending limits.
spanish: Mi responsabilidad es gestionar el presupuesto del proyecto y asegurar que no excedamos nuestros límites de gasto asignados.

text: We must foster a culture of open communication where feedback is shared constructively and {{c1::respectfully}}.
explanation: 'Respectfully' is an adverb meaning in a way that shows honor, politeness, and consideration for other people's feelings and opinions.
usage:
Pattern: respectfully disagree / treat others respectfully
  • You can respectfully disagree with your manager during meetings.
  • Please treat all team members respectfully.
spanish: Debemos fomentar una cultura de comunicación abierta donde la retroalimentación se comparta de manera constructiva y respetuosa.

text: I need to coordinate with the recruitment team to hire additional developers for our new product {{c1::line}}.
explanation: A 'product line' is a group of related products manufactured or distributed by a single company.
usage:
Pattern: product line / launch a product line
  • We are expanding our product line to target enterprise users.
  • He is the director for our software product line.
spanish: Necesito coordinar con el equipo de reclutamiento para contratar desarrolladores adicionales para nuestra nueva línea de productos.

text: We will hold a team retro meeting next Friday to discuss what went well and what we can {{c1::improve}}.
explanation: To 'improve' is the verb meaning to make or become better, a core goal in Agile retrospective (retro) meetings.
usage:
Pattern: continuous improvement / improve code quality
  • We met to discuss how we can improve our deployment workflow.
  • Automation helps improve efficiency.
spanish: Realizaremos una reunión retrospectiva de equipo el próximo viernes para discutir qué salió bien y qué podemos mejorar.

text: I need to review our team's project deliverables and ensure they meet our quality {{c1::assurance standards}}.
explanation: 'Quality assurance standards' (QA standards) are the system of checks and criteria used to verify that software or products are bug-free and meet specifications.
usage:
Pattern: quality assurance standards / QA guidelines
  • All releases must comply with our quality assurance standards.
  • The code was rejected because it failed quality assurance standards.
spanish: Necesito revisar los entregables del proyecto de nuestro equipo y asegurar que cumplan con nuestros estándares de control de calidad.

text: We must set clear boundaries regarding working hours to prevent burnout among our remote {{c1::workers}}.
explanation: A 'remote worker' (or telecommuter) is an employee who works from home or another location outside the physical company office.
usage:
Pattern: remote workers / hybrid work model
  • Managing remote workers requires clear communication channels.
  • Many remote workers appreciate the flexible schedule.
spanish: Debemos establecer límites claros con respecto a las horas de trabajo para evitar el agotamiento entre nuestros trabajadores remotos.

text: I am here to help you succeed; please do not hesitate to reach out if you need any resources or {{c1::guidance}}.
explanation: 'Guidance' refers to advice, direction, or instruction provided by a senior manager or mentor to help a person resolve tasks or grow.
usage:
Pattern: seek guidance / offer guidance
  • Junior developers seek guidance from tech leads.
  • I appreciate your guidance during the code refactoring.
spanish: Estoy aquí para ayudarle a tener éxito; por favor, no dude en comunicarse si necesita algún recurso u orientación.

text: We need to align our technical roadmap with the product manager's business objectives for the upcoming {{c1::year}}.
explanation: An 'upcoming year' (or fiscal year) is the subsequent calendar or financial period for which goals and budgets are planned.
usage:
Pattern: upcoming year / upcoming quarter
  • What are the company goals for the upcoming year?
  • We planned our budget for the upcoming year.
spanish: Necesitamos alinear nuestra hoja de ruta técnica con los objetivos comerciales del gerente de producto para el próximo año.

scenario: Meeting Efficiency: Parking Lot / Tabling Topics 🅿️
text: When a conversation goes off-track, you can say: "In the interest of time, let's {{c1::park this topic}} and {{c2::circle back to it}} at the end of the meeting."
explanation: In the interest of time means to save time. **Park a topic** (or put it in the 'parking lot') means to temporarily postpone discussing it. **Circle back** means to return to a topic later.
usage:
Pattern: In the interest of time, let's park [topic] and circle back to it [time]
  • In the interest of time, let's park the budget discussion and circle back to it on Friday.
  • I want to circle back to what you said about API keys.
spanish: Cuando una conversación se desvía, puedes decir: "Por razones de tiempo, guardemos este tema en el tintero y volvamos a él al final de la reunión".

text: If a detailed discussion only concerns a few people: "This seems to require a deeper dive, so let's {{c1::take this offline}} and let the rest of the team {{c2::get some time back}}."
explanation: Take this offline is an idiom meaning to discuss a topic privately outside of the current meeting. **Get some time back** is a polite way to end a meeting early or release people who are not needed.
usage:
Pattern: take this offline / get [amount of] time back
  • Let's take the database schema discussion offline.
  • If we finish early, everyone gets 10 minutes back.
spanish: Si una discusión detallada solo concierne a unas pocas personas: "Esto parece requerir un análisis más profundo, así que hablemos de esto fuera de línea y dejemos que el resto del equipo recupere algo de tiempo".

text: Let's {{c1::keep this discussion high-level}} today, as we only have 15 minutes left on the agenda.
explanation: Keep high-level means to focus on the big picture, summaries, and general strategies rather than getting caught up in minor details or technicalities.
usage:
Pattern: keep this [discussion/sync] high-level
  • Let's keep this sync high-level and discuss implementation next week.
  • She kept the presentation high-level for the executives.
spanish: Para evitar empantanarse en los detalles: "Mantengamos esta discusión a un nivel general hoy, ya que solo nos quedan 15 minutos en la agenda".

text: I want to make sure we {{c1::stick to the agenda}} so we can {{c2::cover all the items}} we planned for today.
explanation: Stick to the agenda means to follow the pre-planned list of topics. **Cover items** means to discuss and resolve the topics.
usage:
Pattern: stick to the agenda / cover the items
  • If we stick to the agenda, we will finish on time.
  • We have a lot of items to cover in today's standup.
spanish: Quiero asegurarme de que nos apeguemos a la agenda para que podamos cubrir todos los temas que planeamos para hoy.

text: Let's keep this meeting productive by stick to the agenda and avoiding {{c1::going off on tangents}}.
explanation: To 'go off on a tangent' means to suddenly start talking about a subject that is not directly related to the main topic of conversation.
usage:
Pattern: go off on a tangent / deviate from agenda
  • We wasted fifteen minutes because the speaker went off on a tangent.
  • Let's focus on the bug and avoid going off on tangents.
spanish: Mantengamos esta reunión productiva apegándonos a la agenda y evitando irnos por las ramas/desviarnos del tema.

text: We only have thirty minutes, so let's {{c1::get straight to the point}}.
explanation: To 'get straight to the point' means to address the most important matter directly, without wasting time on introductory or irrelevant remarks.
usage:
Pattern: get straight to the point / cut to the chase
  • I'll get straight to the point: our server was hacked.
  • Baristas appreciate it when customers get straight to the point.
spanish: Solo tenemos treinta minutos, así que vayamos directo al grano.

text: Could you please send out the meeting minutes and list the {{c1::action items}} by end of day?
explanation: An 'action item' is a specific task, chore, or action identified during a meeting that must be executed by a designated team member.
usage:
Pattern: action items / list of tasks
  • We assigned the action items to the developers.
  • Please review your action items before the next sync.
spanish: ¿Podrías por favor enviar la minuta de la reunión y listar las tareas pendientes/puntos de acción para el final del día?

text: Let's schedule a brief stand-up meeting tomorrow to check in on our progress and {{c1::realign}}.
explanation: To 'realign' means to adjust or coordinate resources, strategies, or team members back into correct alignment or agreement regarding goals.
usage:
Pattern: realign resources / realign expectations
  • We need to realign our strategy to match the new budget.
  • The team met to realign after the project scope changed.
spanish: Agendemos una breve reunión diaria (stand-up) mañana para revisar nuestro progreso y volver a alinearnos.

text: We need to respect everyone's time and make sure we start and end our meetings {{c1::on schedule}}.
explanation: 'On schedule' (or on time) means at the planned or expected time, according to the timetable or agenda.
usage:
Pattern: on schedule / behind schedule / ahead of schedule
  • The train arrived exactly on schedule.
  • We must complete the deployment on schedule.
spanish: Necesitamos respetar el tiempo de todos y asegurarnos de comenzar y terminar nuestras reuniones a tiempo/según lo programado.

text: Please review the prep materials before the meeting so we can focus on making {{c1::key decisions}}.
explanation: A 'key decision' is a highly important or critical resolution that determines the path, budget, or success of a project or firm.
usage:
Pattern: make key decisions / decisive actions
  • The board met to make key decisions regarding the merger.
  • We need senior management to make key decisions on the architecture.
spanish: Por favor, revise los materiales de preparación antes de la reunión para que podamos enfocarnos en tomar decisiones clave.

text: Let's make sure we stick to the time limits for each agenda item to avoid running {{c1::over time}}.
explanation: To 'run over time' (or run late) means that a meeting or task takes longer than the scheduled duration, disrupting subsequent events.
usage:
Pattern: run over time / run late
  • The presentation ran over time, so we had to skip the Q&A.
  • Please notify the client if you are running late.
spanish: Asegurémonos de apegarnos a los límites de tiempo para cada punto de la agenda para evitar pasarnos del tiempo.

text: We only have ten minutes left, so let's skip the details and focus on the main {{c1::takeaway}}.
explanation: A 'takeaway' (or key learning) is a primary fact, conclusion, or piece of information that you learn or retain from a discussion or meeting.
usage:
Pattern: main takeaway / key takeaway
  • What is the main takeaway from the post-mortem report?
  • My key takeaway was that we must run stress tests before launching.
spanish: Solo nos quedan diez minutos, así que omitamos los detalles y enfoquémonos en la conclusión principal/punto clave.

text: Could you please share the slide deck before the call so everyone can review the data in {{c1::advance}}?
explanation: To do something 'in advance' (or beforehand) means doing it ahead of time, before a specific event or deadline.
usage:
Pattern: in advance / beforehand
  • Please submit your requests two days in advance.
  • I booked the train tickets in advance to save money.
spanish: ¿Podrías por favor compartir la presentación de diapositivas antes de la llamada para que todos puedan revisar los datos con anticipación?

text: Let's schedule a follow-up meeting next week to review progress on our action {{c1::items}}.
explanation: 'Action items' are the specific tasks assigned to individuals during a meeting, here referenced in the plural form.
usage:
Pattern: action items / list of tasks
  • We documented all action items in the shared document.
  • Let's make sure we execute our action items on schedule.
spanish: Agendemos una reunión de seguimiento la próxima semana para revisar el progreso de nuestras tareas pendientes.

text: We need to respect the meeting host's agenda and avoid introducing unrelated topics without prior {{c1::notice}}.
explanation: 'Prior notice' (or advance warning) is a notification given ahead of time regarding a change, request, or event.
usage:
Pattern: prior notice / short notice
  • You must give two weeks' prior notice before taking vacation.
  • Thank you for attending on such short notice.
spanish: Necesitamos respetar la agenda del anfitrión de la reunión y evitar introducir temas no relacionados sin previo aviso.

text: Please make sure your microphone is muted when you are not speaking to minimize background {{c1::noise}}.
explanation: 'Background noise' is any extraneous, unwanted sound (like traffic, construction, or typing) that interferes with communication during a call.
usage:
Pattern: background noise / extraneous sound
  • There was too much background noise during the call.
  • Use a headset to reduce background noise.
spanish: Por favor, asegúrese de silenciar su micrófono cuando no esté hablando para minimizar el ruido de fondo.

text: If you have any detailed feedback, please send it via email to avoid holding up the rest of the {{c1::group}}.
explanation: To 'hold up' the group is a phrasal verb meaning to delay, block, or slow down progress for everyone in the meeting.
usage:
Pattern: hold up the process / delay the team
  • We don't want to hold up the release, so deploy the hotfix now.
  • The accident held up traffic for hours.
spanish: Si tiene algún comentario detallado, envíelo por correo electrónico para evitar retrasar al resto del grupo.

text: Let's take this discussion offline and schedule a separate call with the database engineers to {{c1::discuss the details}}.
explanation: To 'discuss the details' is the standard collocation meaning to evaluate the specifics, technical details, or fine points of a plan.
usage:
Pattern: discuss the details / evaluate specifics
  • We met to discuss the details of the API integration.
  • The post-mortem report discusses the details of the crash.
spanish: Llevemos esta discusión fuera de la reunión (offline) y programemos una llamada separada con los ingenieros de bases de datos para discutir los detalles.

text: We have reached our goals for this meeting, so let's adjourn and get back to our daily {{c1::tasks}}.
explanation: 'Daily tasks' (or daily chores/duties) are the routine activities that an employee must execute each day in their job.
usage:
Pattern: daily tasks / routine workload
  • Writing unit tests is one of my daily tasks.
  • He organized his daily tasks on a Kanban board.
spanish: Hemos logrado nuestros objetivos para esta reunión, así que levantemos la sesión y volvamos a nuestras tareas diarias.

text: Thank you all for joining today; I will share the recording of this session with the team members who were {{c1::absent}}.
explanation: 'Absent' is an adjective meaning not present in a place, meeting, or event.
usage:
Pattern: absent from work / absent members
  • He was absent from the meeting because of illness.
  • The secretary noted the names of the absent board members.
spanish: Gracias a todos por unirse hoy; compartiré la grabación de esta sesión con los miembros del equipo que estuvieron ausentes.

text: We cannot agree to these terms unless you include a {{c1::termination clause}} for convenience.
explanation: A termination clause is a contract provision that defines the conditions under which the agreement can be ended. For convenience means it can be terminated without needing a specific cause or breach.
usage:
Pattern: termination clause / terminate for convenience
  • The contract contains a 30-day termination clause.
  • Either party may terminate the agreement for convenience with written notice.
spanish: No podemos aceptar estos términos a menos que incluya una cláusula de rescisión por conveniencia.

text: During the review, he negotiated: "Based on the {{c1::market rate}} and my contributions, I'd like to adjust my {{c2::compensation package}} to include remote-work fringe benefits."
explanation: Market rate is the standard salary for a role. **Compensation package** includes base salary, bonuses, and benefits. **Fringe benefits** are extra benefits (like gym memberships or health insurance).
usage:
Pattern: negotiate a compensation package based on market rate / include fringe benefits
  • Our company offers an attractive compensation package with great fringe benefits.
  • The offer is below the current market rate for senior developers.
spanish: Durante la revisión, negoció: "Basándome en la tarifa del mercado y mis contribuciones, me gustaría ajustar mi paquete de compensación para incluir beneficios adicionales de trabajo remoto".

text: We are willing to make concessions, but we need you to match us on the {{c1::payment terms}}.
explanation: 'Payment terms' are the conditions under which a seller will complete a sale, specifying when payment is due and any discounts or fees (e.g. Net 30).
usage:
Pattern: payment terms / Net 30 / Net 60
  • The contract specifies payment terms of Net 45.
  • We negotiated better payment terms with our main vendor.
spanish: Estamos dispuestos a hacer concesiones, pero necesitamos que esté de acuerdo con nosotros en las condiciones de pago.

text: Our goal is to reach a win-win agreement that benefits both parties in the {{c1::long run}}.
explanation: The 'long run' is an idiomatic expression referring to a long period of time in the future, as opposed to the immediate present.
usage:
Pattern: in the long run / long-term
  • This software is expensive, but it will save us money in the long run.
  • Our decisions must benefit the project in the long run.
spanish: Nuestra meta es llegar a un acuerdo de beneficio mutuo que beneficie a ambas partes a largo plazo.

text: We cannot accept these terms; they do not align with our risk management {{c1::guidelines}}.
explanation: 'Guidelines' are official rules, principles, or instructions that dictate how a task or business policy must be conducted.
usage:
Pattern: guidelines / company policy
  • Please review the branding guidelines before making the logo.
  • Our risk management guidelines prohibit this setup.
spanish: No podemos aceptar estas condiciones; no se alinean con nuestras directrices/pautas de gestión de riesgos.

text: Let's discuss the pricing structure: is there any room for a discount on {{c1::bulk orders}}?
explanation: A 'bulk order' (or bulk purchase) is an order for a very large quantity of a product, which typically qualifies for a discount.
usage:
Pattern: bulk order / bulk discount
  • We save 20% on paper supplies by placing a bulk order.
  • The supplier offers a discount for bulk orders.
spanish: Hablemos de la estructura de precios: ¿hay algún margen para un descuento en pedidos al por mayor?

text: We value our partnership and want to find a solution that doesn't {{c1::compromise quality}}.
explanation: To 'compromise quality' means to accept a lower standard or degrade the quality of a product or service, often to save money or time.
usage:
Pattern: compromise quality / lower standards
  • We want to reduce costs, but we will never compromise quality.
  • Cutting budget now might compromise the quality of the release.
spanish: Valoramos nuestra asociación y queremos encontrar una solución que no comprometa la calidad.

text: Could we extend the contract duration in exchange for a lower annual service {{c1::fee}}?
explanation: A 'service fee' is a charge made by a business for performing work or services for a client (distinct from product cost).
usage:
Pattern: service fee / annual fee
  • The contract includes an annual maintenance service fee.
  • The bank charges a service fee for international transfers.
spanish: ¿Podríamos extender la duración del contrato a cambio de una tarifa/cuota de servicio anual más baja?

text: Before we sign the agreement, we need to consult with our legal counsel to {{c1::review the clauses}}.
explanation: To 'review the clauses' means to read and evaluate the specific sections, paragraphs, or provisions of a legal contract to ensure they are acceptable.
usage:
Pattern: review the clauses / contract clauses
  • Our legal team needs to review the liability clauses.
  • We requested changes to the termination clauses.
spanish: Antes de firmar el acuerdo, necesitamos consultar con nuestro asesor legal para revisar las cláusulas.

text: We have reached an agreement on the scope, so let's move forward and {{c1::finalize the contract}}.
explanation: To 'finalize the contract' means to complete the last details of a legal agreement and prepare it for signing by both parties.
usage:
Pattern: finalize the contract / sign contract
  • We are meeting tomorrow to finalize the contract.
  • Once we finalize the contract, we can begin production.
spanish: Hemos llegado a un acuerdo sobre el alcance, así que sigamos adelante y finalicemos el contrato.

text: We are open to negotiating the contract price if you can commit to a multi-year {{c1::service agreement}}.
explanation: A 'service agreement' (or service contract) is a legal document defining the terms, pricing, and support details for services provided by a vendor.
usage:
Pattern: service agreement / SLA contract
  • We signed a three-year service agreement with the cloud provider.
  • The service agreement guarantees next-day hardware replacement.
spanish: Estamos abiertos a negociar el precio del contrato si puede comprometerse con un acuerdo de servicio plurianual.

text: Our objective is to build a long-term partnership based on mutual trust and shared {{c1::goals}}.
explanation: 'Shared goals' are objectives or aims that are held in common by two partnering companies or departments, facilitating collaboration.
usage:
Pattern: shared goals / common objectives
  • Partnerships succeed when both companies have shared goals.
  • Let's define our shared goals before launching the joint venture.
spanish: Nuestro objetivo es construir una asociación a largo plazo basada en la confianza mutua y los objetivos compartidos.

text: We cannot agree to these terms; they place too much financial risk on our {{c1::company}}.
explanation: A 'company' (or corporation) is a commercial business or legal entity formed by a group of people to engage in trade.
usage:
Pattern: our company / corporate entity
  • Our company is expanding its operations in Europe.
  • The merger consolidated the two companies into one.
spanish: No podemos aceptar estas condiciones; representan demasiado riesgo financiero para nuestra empresa.

text: Let's discuss the delivery timeline: can you guarantee delivery within thirty days of signing the {{c1::contract}}?
explanation: A 'contract' is a written or spoken agreement, especially one concerning employment, sales, or tenancy, that is intended to be enforceable by law.
usage:
Pattern: sign the contract / draft a contract
  • Make sure you read the fine print before you sign the contract.
  • We signed the contract yesterday.
spanish: Hablemos del cronograma de entrega: ¿puede garantizar la entrega dentro de los treinta días posteriores a la firma del contrato?

text: We understand your constraints, but we must protect our profit margins to sustain our operations {{c1::long term}}.
explanation: 'Long term' refers to a long period of time in the future, especially regarding business planning or financial returns.
usage:
Pattern: long term / short term
  • Our strategy focuses on long-term sustainability rather than quick profits.
  • This setup is not viable long term.
spanish: Entendemos sus limitaciones, pero debemos proteger nuestros márgenes de beneficio para sostener nuestras operaciones a largo plazo.

text: Could we reduce the payment frequency in exchange for a slightly lower interest {{c1::rate}}?
explanation: An 'interest rate' is the percentage of a loan amount charged by a lender to a borrower for the use of assets.
usage:
Pattern: interest rate / annual interest rate
  • The bank increased its prime interest rate.
  • We negotiated a fixed interest rate for the business loan.
spanish: ¿Podríamos reducir la frecuencia de pago a cambio de una tasa de interés ligeramente más baja?

text: Before we sign the contract, we need to verify that all clauses are compliant with local {{c1::regulations}}.
explanation: 'Regulations' are rules or directives made and maintained by an authority (like a government agency) governing business practices.
usage:
Pattern: compliance regulations / government regulations
  • Our data servers comply with all European regulations (GDPR).
  • Failing to follow environmental regulations leads to heavy fines.
spanish: Antes de firmar el contrato, necesitamos verificar que todas las cláusulas cumplan con las regulaciones locales.

text: We have reached an agreement on all key points; let's draft the final contract and sign it {{c1::tomorrow}}.
explanation: 'Tomorrow' is the day after today, commonly set as a target date in contract negotiations once terms are aligned.
usage:
Pattern: sign it tomorrow / schedule for tomorrow
  • The documents will be ready for signing tomorrow morning.
  • Let's meet tomorrow to review the feedback.
spanish: Hemos llegado a un acuerdo en todos los puntos clave; redactemos el contrato final y firmémoslo mañana.

text: We are willing to compromise on the delivery method if you can cover the shipping {{c1::costs}}.
explanation: 'Shipping costs' are the expenses required to package and transport physical goods from the seller to the buyer.
usage:
Pattern: shipping costs / freight charges
  • The online price does not include shipping costs.
  • We offer free shipping costs for orders over $500.
spanish: Estamos dispuestos a ceder en el método de entrega si usted puede cubrir los costos de envío.

text: Let's review the termination clause: what is the notice period required to end the contract without {{c1::penalties}}?
explanation: A 'penalty' is a punishment or financial fine imposed by contract terms for failing to comply with a clause (such as early termination).
usage:
Pattern: contract penalties / cancellation fee
  • The contract includes financial penalties for late delivery.
  • You can cancel the service after one year without penalties.
spanish: Revisemos la cláusula de rescisión: ¿cuál es el período de notificación requerido para finalizar el contrato sin penalizaciones?

text: Let's {{c1::touch base}} tomorrow to discuss the updates.
explanation: touch base is a common business idiom meaning to briefly contact or meet with someone to talk about progress.
usage:
Pattern: touch base with [someone] [time]
  • I need to touch base with my manager before the client call.
  • Let's touch base next week.
spanish: Póngamonos en contacto mañana para discutir las actualizaciones.

text: We need to {{c1::push back}} the meeting to 3 PM.
explanation: push back means to postpone or reschedule an event to a later time or date.
usage:
Pattern: push back [event] to [time/date]
  • Can we push back the project deadline by two days?
  • They had to push back the launch due to technical issues.
spanish: Necesitamos posponer la reunión para las 3 PM.

text: I'll {{c1::follow up with}} you via email once I get the data.
explanation: follow up with means to contact someone again to provide further information, verify progress, or request updates.
usage:
Pattern: follow up with [someone] on/via [method]
  • The doctor will follow up with you next month.
  • I will follow up with the client on the contract details tomorrow.
spanish: Le daré seguimiento por correo electrónico una vez que obtenga los datos.

text: During the planning sync, the director shared: "Our {{c1::financial forecast}} projects new {{c2::revenue streams}} from our enterprise API licensing model."
explanation: Financial forecast is a projection of future financial performance. **Revenue streams** refer to different sources of income for a business.
usage:
Pattern: financial forecast projects [metric] / identify new revenue streams
  • The financial forecast projects a 15% increase in sales.
  • We are exploring new revenue streams to diversify our income.
spanish: Durante la sincronización de planificación, el director compartió: "Nuestro pronóstico financiero proyecta nuevas fuentes de ingresos a partir de nuestro modelo de licencia de API empresarial".

text: Could you please take notes during the presentation and share them with the team {{c1::afterwards}}?
explanation: 'Afterwards' is an adverb meaning at a later or subsequent time; after something else has occurred.
usage:
Pattern: afterwards / after the meeting
  • We had a brief discussion afterwards.
  • She completed the task and left immediately afterwards.
spanish: ¿Podrías por favor tomar notas durante la presentación y compartirlas con el equipo después/posteriormente?

text: Let's go around the table and have everyone briefly introduce themselves and their {{c1::area of expertise}}.
explanation: An 'area of expertise' is the specific field, subject, or skill set in which a person is highly knowledgeable or skilled.
usage:
Pattern: area of expertise / domain knowledge
  • Database performance tuning is my main area of expertise.
  • We hired consultants with expertise in cloud migration.
spanish: Hagamos una ronda en la mesa para que todos se presenten brevemente y mencionen su área de especialización.

text: I'd like to share my screen to show you the latest mockup designs for the {{c1::landing page}}.
explanation: A 'landing page' is the specific web page that a user arrives at after clicking a link or advertisement, designed to convert visitors.
usage:
Pattern: landing page / homepage
  • We optimized the landing page to increase sign-ups.
  • The marketing campaign directs traffic to a custom landing page.
spanish: Me gustaría compartir mi pantalla para mostrarles los últimos diseños de maqueta para la página de destino (inicio).

text: Let's reserve the last ten minutes of the meeting for a quick Q&A session to address any {{c1::remaining questions}}.
explanation: A 'Q&A session' (Question and Answer) is a dedicated time during a meeting or presentation for the audience to ask questions of the speaker.
usage:
Pattern: Q&A session / questions and answers
  • The webinar ended with a lively Q&A session.
  • We will take questions during the Q&A session at the end.
spanish: Reservemos los últimos diez minutos de la reunión para una sesión rápida de preguntas y respuestas para abordar cualquier pregunta restante.

text: I agree with your suggestion, let's incorporate that feedback into the next iteration of the {{c1::proposal}}.
explanation: A 'proposal' is a formal written document or offer presented to others for consideration, typically detailing a plan, budget, or contract.
usage:
Pattern: project proposal / business proposal
  • We submitted our proposal to the client yesterday.
  • The board will review the proposal at the next meeting.
spanish: Estoy de acuerdo con su sugerencia, incorporemos esa retroalimentación en la próxima iteración de la propuesta.

text: Before we begin, let's quickly review the {{c1::agenda items}} to make sure we allocate our time properly.
explanation: Agenda items are the individual topics or tasks scheduled to be discussed during a meeting.
usage:
Pattern: agenda items / meeting agenda
  • We have five agenda items to cover today.
  • Please add this topic to the agenda items for next week.
spanish: Antes de comenzar, revisemos rápidamente los puntos de la agenda para asegurarnos de asignar nuestro tiempo adecuadamente.

text: Could you please take minutes during the meeting and email them to the attendees {{c1::by tomorrow morning}}?
explanation: 'By tomorrow morning' is a common business deadline, requesting that a task be completed and sent before the start of the next workday.
usage:
Pattern: by tomorrow morning / first thing tomorrow
  • I'll have the code review ready by tomorrow morning.
  • Please send the contract to the client by tomorrow morning.
spanish: ¿Podrías por favor tomar la minuta durante la reunión y enviarla por correo electrónico a los asistentes para mañana por la mañana?

text: Let's introduce our new team member, Colin, who will be taking over the database migration {{c1::project}}.
explanation: A 'project' is an individual or collaborative enterprise that is carefully planned and designed to achieve a particular aim.
usage:
Pattern: project manager / project milestones
  • I am managing the new API development project.
  • The database migration project was completed on schedule.
spanish: Presentemos a nuestro nuevo miembro del equipo, Colin, quien se hará cargo del proyecto de migración de la base de datos.

text: I'd like to present the user flow diagram to show how the customer registers on our {{c1::platform}}.
explanation: A 'platform' is a major software environment, website, or infrastructure where services are hosted and executed.
usage:
Pattern: software platform / cloud platform
  • Our platform supports over 10,000 concurrent connections.
  • We deployed our application on a secure hosting platform.
spanish: Me gustaría presentar el diagrama de flujo de usuario para mostrar cómo el cliente se registra en nuestra plataforma.

text: We will allocate the last fifteen minutes of the meeting for an open discussion to address any {{c1::concerns}}.
explanation: A 'concern' is a matter of interest, anxiety, or worry raised by team members or clients regarding a plan or change.
usage:
Pattern: address concerns / voice concerns
  • The director met with staff to address their concerns about the restructuring.
  • If you have any safety concerns, please voice them immediately.
spanish: Asignaremos los últimos quince minutos de la reunión para una discusión abierta para abordar cualquier preocupación.

text: I agree with your proposal; let's schedule a follow-up call to plan the implementation {{c1::steps}}.
explanation: 'Steps' refer to the phases or individual tasks required to carry out a proposal or plan.
usage:
Pattern: implementation steps / action steps
  • The document outlines the implementation steps for the database migration.
  • We followed the configuration steps exactly.
spanish: Estoy de acuerdo con su propuesta; programemos una llamada de seguimiento para planificar los pasos de implementación.

text: Please make sure you have the report ready before the meeting starts so we can reference the {{c1::metrics}}.
explanation: 'Metrics' are measures of quantitative assessment commonly used for assessing, comparing, and tracking performance (e.g. latency, conversions).
usage:
Pattern: performance metrics / tracking metrics
  • Our primary performance metric is API response time.
  • Review the marketing metrics before launching the campaign.
spanish: Por favor, asegúrese de tener el informe listo antes de que comience la reunión para que podamos hacer referencia a las métricas.

text: If there are no objections, let's proceed with the plan and assign tasks to the respective team {{c1::members}}.
explanation: A 'member' is an individual belonging to a group or team.
usage:
Pattern: team members / staff members
  • All team members must attend the daily stand-up meeting.
  • We welcomed three new members to our division.
spanish: Si no hay objeciones, procedamos con el plan y asignemos tareas a los respectivos miembros del equipo.

text: I'd like to share a quick update on our customer acquisition costs for this past {{c1::quarter}}.
explanation: A 'quarter' (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4) is a three-month period in a financial calendar, used to report business earnings and metrics.
usage:
Pattern: fiscal quarter / third quarter / Q3
  • Our revenue increased by 10% in the last quarter.
  • We are planning our goals for the upcoming quarter.
spanish: Me gustaría compartir una actualización rápida sobre nuestros costos de adquisición de clientes para este último trimestre.

text: Let's keep the discussion focused on the project milestones and avoid discussing future product {{c1::features}}.
explanation: A 'feature' is a distinctive attribute or aspect of a software application (e.g. user authentication, dark mode).
usage:
Pattern: product features / new features
  • The developer is coding the new search feature.
  • Users requested a data export feature.
spanish: Mantengamos la discusión enfocada en los hitos del proyecto y evitemos discutir futuras características del producto.

text: Thank you all for your feedback today; we will use these insights to refine the project design before our next {{c1::sync}}.
explanation: A 'sync' (synchronization) is business jargon for a regular meeting scheduled to align team members on project updates.
usage:
Pattern: weekly sync / schedule a sync
  • Let's schedule a quick sync call tomorrow morning.
  • The weekly sync is scheduled for Tuesdays at 10:00 AM.
spanish: Gracias a todos por sus comentarios de hoy; utilizaremos estos conocimientos para refinar el diseño del proyecto antes de nuestra próxima reunión de sincronización.

text: Let's not {{c1::get bogged down in}} the implementation details during this high-level sync.
explanation: get bogged down in means to become so stuck or overwhelmed by small details that you lose sight of the main goal and stop making progress.
usage:
Pattern: get bogged down in [details/logistics/work]
  • Don't get bogged down in minor edits; focus on completing the draft.
  • Our discussion got bogged down in budget arguments.
spanish: No nos empantanemos en los detalles de implementación durante esta sincronización de alto nivel.

text: Welcome everyone; let's start by reviewing the agenda for today's call and identifying our {{c1::key objectives}}.
explanation: 'Key objectives' are the primary, most important goals or outcomes that a meeting or project is designed to achieve.
usage:
Pattern: key objectives / main goals
  • The key objectives of this phase are database cleanup and indexing.
  • What are the key objectives for today's meeting?
spanish: Bienvenidos todos; comencemos revisando la agenda para la llamada de hoy e identificando nuestros objetivos clave.

text: I'll be facilitating the discussion today, and I want to make sure everyone has an opportunity to {{c1::voice their opinion}}.
explanation: To 'voice your opinion' means to express your thoughts, beliefs, or views on a subject openly and publicly.
usage:
Pattern: voice opinion / express views
  • Employees are encouraged to voice their opinions in team surveys.
  • He was not afraid to voice his opinion during the board meeting.
spanish: Estaré moderando la discusión hoy, y quiero asegurarme de que todos tengan la oportunidad de expresar su opinión.

text: Let's keep an eye on the time: we need to move on to the next topic on the agenda in {{c1::five minutes}}.
explanation: To 'keep an eye on the time' means to monitor the clock continuously to ensure the meeting doesn't run late.
usage:
Pattern: keep an eye on the time / track time
  • We must keep an eye on the time to avoid missing our flight.
  • I am keeping an eye on the time so we finish before noon.
spanish: Estemos atentos al tiempo: necesitamos pasar al siguiente tema de la agenda en cinco minutos.

text: Could you please keep your updates concise so we have enough time to discuss the new {{c1::marketing campaign}}?
explanation: A 'marketing campaign' is an organized, strategic course of action to promote a product, service, or brand across media channels.
usage:
Pattern: marketing campaign / advertising campaign
  • We are launching a new marketing campaign next week.
  • The marketing campaign resulted in a 30% increase in traffic.
spanish: ¿Podría por favor mantener sus actualizaciones breves para que tengamos suficiente tiempo para discutir la nueva campaña de marketing?

text: Let's summarize the main points we've agreed on before we adjourn the meeting for {{c1::today}}.
explanation: To 'adjourn the meeting' is formal language meaning to temporarily end or suspend a meeting or assembly.
usage:
Pattern: adjourn the meeting / end the session
  • The committee decided to adjourn the meeting until next Tuesday.
  • The chairman adjourned the meeting at 4:00 PM.
spanish: Resumamos los puntos principales en los que hemos estado de acuerdo antes de levantar la sesión por hoy.

text: If there are no objections, let's approve the previous meeting minutes and move to the {{c1::first item}}.
explanation: To 'approve the minutes' is the formal process in business meetings of verifying that the written record of the previous meeting is accurate.
usage:
Pattern: approve the minutes / review minutes
  • The board voted to approve the minutes of the January meeting.
  • First, we must approve the minutes before discussing new business.
spanish: Si no hay objeciones, aprobemos la minuta de la reunión anterior y pasemos al primer punto.

text: I'd like to invite our guest speaker to share their insights on industry trends and customer {{c1::behavior}}.
explanation: To 'share insights' means to explain deep understandings, professional knowledge, or analytical conclusions about a topic to others.
usage:
Pattern: share insights / offer recommendations
  • The analyst shared valuable insights on market growth.
  • Thank you for sharing your insights with us today.
spanish: Me gustaría invitar a nuestro orador invitado a compartir sus conocimientos/perspectivas sobre las tendencias de la industria y el comportamiento del cliente.

text: Let's stay focused on the topic at hand; we can discuss other issues during our next scheduled {{c1::sync call}}.
explanation: The 'topic at hand' is the specific subject, problem, or agenda item currently being discussed or addressed.
usage:
Pattern: topic at hand / current subject
  • Please limit your questions to the topic at hand.
  • The manager guided the team back to the topic at hand.
spanish: Mantengámonos enfocados en el tema en cuestión; podemos discutir otros asuntos durante nuestra próxima llamada de sincronización programada.

text: Thank you for your active participation today; I will send out a follow-up email with the next steps {{c1::shortly}}.
explanation: 'Active participation' refers to actively engaging, speaking, and contributing value during a group meeting or workshop.
usage:
Pattern: active participation / engage in discussion
  • The success of the brainstorming session depended on everyone's active participation.
  • Thank you all for your active participation today.
spanish: Gracias por su participación activa hoy; enviaré un correo electrónico de seguimiento con los siguientes pasos en breve.

text: Let's start the meeting on time; we have a lot of topics to cover on our agenda {{c1::today}}.
explanation: 'Today' refers to the present day, commonly used to set context at the start of a business call.
usage:
Pattern: on our agenda today / schedule for today
  • What is the main topic on our agenda today?
  • We completed all tasks scheduled for today.
spanish: Comencemos la reunión a tiempo; tenemos muchos temas que cubrir en nuestra agenda hoy.

text: I'll be leading the meeting today; please let me know if anyone has any updates to add to the {{c1::agenda}}.
explanation: An 'agenda' is a list of items or topics to be discussed or resolved during a formal meeting.
usage:
Pattern: meeting agenda / items on the agenda
  • Please review the agenda before the call starts.
  • The manager added two new points to the meeting agenda.
spanish: Estaré guiando la reunión hoy; por favor, avísenme si alguien tiene alguna actualización que agregar a la agenda.

text: Let's keep our updates brief so we have enough time to discuss the database migration {{c1::plan}}.
explanation: A 'plan' is a detailed proposal for doing or achieving something, here referencing the technical migration plan.
usage:
Pattern: migration plan / project plan
  • The architect drafted a detailed migration plan.
  • We must follow the rollout plan to avoid service disruption.
spanish: Mantengamos nuestras actualizaciones breves para que tengamos suficiente tiempo para discutir el plan de migración de la base de datos.

text: Could you please summarize your findings in two sentences to help us stay on {{c1::schedule}}?
explanation: To 'stay on schedule' is the business collocation meaning to complete tasks or run meetings within the planned timetable.
usage:
Pattern: stay on schedule / keep on schedule
  • We adjusted the timeline to ensure we stay on schedule.
  • The project manager is responsible for keeping the team on schedule.
spanish: ¿Podrías por favor resumir tus hallazgos en dos oraciones para ayudarnos a mantenernos según lo programado?

text: Let's review the action items we agreed on before we close the meeting for {{c1::this afternoon}}?
explanation: 'This afternoon' refers to the latter part of the present day, commonly set as a deadline or closure time for calls.
usage:
Pattern: this afternoon / late afternoon
  • The migration is scheduled for this afternoon.
  • I will send the report this afternoon.
spanish: ¿Revisamos las tareas pendientes en las que estuvimos de acuerdo antes de cerrar la reunión de esta tarde?

text: If there are no objections, let's approve the previous meeting minutes and move to our first {{c1::topic}}.
explanation: A 'topic' is a subject or theme of discussion or study in a meeting.
usage:
Pattern: first topic / main topic / subject of discussion
  • The first topic on our agenda is database latency.
  • Let's move to the next topic.
spanish: Si no hay objeciones, aprobemos la minuta de la reunión anterior y pasemos a nuestro primer tema.

text: I'd like to invite our product manager to present the new features scheduled for the next product {{c1::release}}.
explanation: A 'release' (or deployment) is the distribution of a new version of software to production.
usage:
Pattern: product release / next release
  • We are coding the features for the next release.
  • The code is scheduled for the upcoming release.
spanish: Me gustaría invitar a nuestro gerente de producto a presentar las nuevas características programadas para el próximo lanzamiento del producto.

text: Let's stay focused on the project milestones; we can discuss other issues in our weekly {{c1::check-in call}}.
explanation: A 'check-in call' (or status sync) is a regular, brief meeting to monitor progress and coordinate tasks.
usage:
Pattern: weekly check-in call / daily check-in
  • The manager schedules a check-in call every Monday.
  • Let's use the check-in call to coordinate our updates.
spanish: Mantengámonos enfocados en los hitos del proyecto; podemos discutir otros asuntos en nuestra llamada de control semanal.

text: Thank you all for joining; I will send out a follow-up email with the meeting notes and action items {{c1::shortly}}.
explanation: 'Shortly' is an adverb meaning in a short time from now; soon, indicating rapid follow-up action.
usage:
Pattern: shortly after / shortly before / arrive shortly
  • The service was restored shortly after the reboot.
  • The manager will join the call shortly.
spanish: Gracias a todos por unirse; enviaré un correo electrónico de seguimiento con las notas de la reunión y las tareas pendientes en breve.

text: If anyone has any remaining questions, please post them on our Slack channel to avoid running {{c1::over time}}.
explanation: To run 'over time' (or exceed schedule) is the phrasal verb meaning to take longer than the allocated meeting duration.
usage:
Pattern: running over time / exceed time limits
  • We must end the call now to avoid running over time.
  • The workshop ran over time by thirty minutes.
spanish: Si alguien tiene alguna pregunta restante, por favor publíquela en nuestro canal de Slack para evitar pasarnos de tiempo.

text: When a customer is upset about an outage, you can say: "I completely {{c1::understand how frustrating}} it must be to {{c2::have your workflow disrupted}}."
explanation: This phrase validates the customer's feelings and shows you understand the impact of the issue on their business, which immediately lowers tension.
usage:
